message.content:
Look at https://docs.github.com/en/actions/concepts/security/artifact-attestations and add attestations to moltis, the same way secretive does: https://github.com/maxgoedjen/secretive

You can also link attestations in README.md and the documentation, in a security sections, where is documentation about me signing packages with my gpg key, and cosign.

2→ 3→ 4→ 5→# Moltis — A secure persistent personal agent server in Rust 6→ 7→One binary — sandboxed, secure, yours. 8→ 9→[![CI](https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/actions/workflows/ci.yml) 10→[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/moltis-org/moltis/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/moltis-org/moltis) 11→[![CodSpeed](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://codspeed.io/badge.json&style=flat&label=CodSpeed)](https://codspeed.io/moltis-org/moltis) 12→[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) 13→[![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/Rust-1.91%2B-orange.svg)](https://www.rust-lang.org) 14→[![Discord](https://img.shields.io/discord/1469505370169933837?color=5865F2&label=Discord&logo=discord&logoColor=white)](https://discord.gg/XnmrepsXp5) 15→ 16→[Installation](#installation) • [Comparison](#comparison) • [Architecture](#architecture--crate-map) • [Security](#security) • [Features](#features) • [How It Works](#how-it-works) • [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md) 17→ 18→
19→ 20→--- 21→ 22→Moltis recently hit [the front page of Hacker News](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=46993587). Please [open an issue](https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/issues) for any friction at all. I'm focused on making Moltis excellent. 23→ 24→**Secure by design** — Your keys never leave your machine. Every command runs in a sandboxed container, never on your host. 25→ 26→**Your hardware** — Runs on a Mac Mini, a Raspberry Pi, or any server you own. One Rust binary, no Node.js, no npm, no runtime. 27→ 28→**Full-featured** — Voice, memory, cross-session recall, automatic edit checkpoints, scheduling, Telegram, Discord, browser automation, MCP servers, SSH or node-backed remote exec, managed deploy keys with host pinning in the web UI, a live Settings → Tools inventory, Cursor-compatible project context, and context-file threat scanning — all built-in. No plugin marketplace to get supply-chain attacked through. 29→ 30→**Auditable** — The agent loop + provider model fits in ~5K lines. The core (excluding the optional web UI) is ~196K lines across 46 modular crates you can audit independently, with 3,100+ tests and zero `unsafe` code\*. 31→ 32→## Installation 33→ 34→```bash 35→# One-liner install script (macOS / Linux) 36→curl -fsSL https://www.moltis.org/install.sh | sh 37→ 38→# macOS / Linux via Homebrew 39→brew install moltis-org/tap/moltis 40→ 41→# Docker (multi-arch: amd64/arm64) 42→docker pull ghcr.io/moltis-org/moltis:latest 43→ 44→# Or build from source 45→cargo install moltis --git https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis 46→``` 47→ 48→## Comparison 49→ 50→| | OpenClaw | PicoClaw | NanoClaw | ZeroClaw | **Moltis** | 51→|---|---|---|---|---|---| 52→| Language | TypeScript | Go | TypeScript | Rust | **Rust** | 53→| Agent loop | ~430K LoC | Small | ~500 LoC | ~3.4K LoC | **~5K LoC** (`runner.rs` + `model.rs`) | 54→| Full codebase | — | — | — | 1,000+ tests | **~124K LoC** (2,300+ tests) | 55→| Runtime | Node.js + npm | Single binary | Node.js | Single binary (3.4 MB) | **Single binary (44 MB)** | 56→| Sandbox | App-level | — | Docker | Docker | **Docker + Apple Container** | 57→| Memory safety | GC | GC | GC | Ownership | **Ownership, zero `unsafe`\*** | 58→| Auth | Basic | API keys | None | Token + OAuth | **Password + Passkey + API keys + Vault** | 59→| Voice I/O | Plugin | — | — | — | **Built-in (15+ providers)** | 60→| MCP | Yes | — | — | — | **Yes (stdio + HTTP/SSE)** | 61→| Hooks | Yes (limited) | — | — | — | **15 event types** | 62→| Skills | Yes (store) | Yes | Yes | Yes | **Yes (+ OpenClaw Store)** | 63→| Memory/RAG | Plugin | — | Per-group | SQLite + FTS | **SQLite + FTS + vector** | 64→ 65→\* `unsafe` is denied workspace-wide. The only exceptions are opt-in FFI wrappers behind the `local-embeddings` feature flag, not part of the core. 66→ 67→> [Full comparison with benchmarks →](https://docs.moltis.org/comparison.html) 68→ 69→## Architecture — Crate Map 70→ 71→**Core** (always compiled): 72→ 73→| Crate | LoC | Role | 74→|-------|-----|------| 75→| `moltis` (cli) | 4.0K | Entry point, CLI commands | 76→| `moltis-agents` | 9.6K | Agent loop, streaming, prompt assembly | 77→| `moltis-providers` | 17.6K | LLM provider implementations | 78→| `moltis-gateway` | 36.1K | HTTP/WS server, RPC, auth | 79→| `moltis-chat` | 11.5K | Chat engine, agent orchestration | 80→| `moltis-tools` | 21.9K | Tool execution, sandbox | 81→| `moltis-config` | 7.0K | Configuration, validation | 82→| `moltis-sessions` | 3.8K | Session persistence | 83→| `moltis-plugins` | 1.9K | Hook dispatch, plugin formats | 84→| `moltis-service-traits` | 1.3K | Shared service interfaces | 85→| `moltis-common` | 1.1K | Shared utilities | 86→| `moltis-protocol` | 0.8K | Wire protocol types | 87→ 88→**Optional** (feature-gated or additive): 89→ 90→| Category | Crates | Combined LoC | 91→|----------|--------|-------------| 92→| Web UI | `moltis-web` | 4.5K | 93→| GraphQL | `moltis-graphql` | 4.8K | 94→| Voice | `moltis-voice` | 6.0K | 95→| Memory | `moltis-memory`, `moltis-qmd` | 5.9K | 96→| Channels | `moltis-telegram`, `moltis-whatsapp`, `moltis-discord`, `moltis-msteams`, `moltis-channels` | 14.9K | 97→| Browser | `moltis-browser` | 5.1K | 98→| Scheduling | `moltis-cron`, `moltis-caldav` | 5.2K | 99→| Extensibility | `moltis-mcp`, `moltis-skills`, `moltis-wasm-tools` | 9.1K | 100→| Auth & Security | `moltis-auth`, `moltis-oauth`, `moltis-onboarding`, `moltis-vault` | 6.6K | 101→| Networking | `moltis-network-filter`, `moltis-tls`, `moltis-tailscale` | 3.5K | 102→| Provider setup | `moltis-provider-setup` | 4.3K | 103→| Import | `moltis-openclaw-import` | 7.6K | 104→| Apple native | `moltis-swift-bridge` | 2.1K | 105→| Metrics | `moltis-metrics` | 1.7K | 106→| Other | `moltis-projects`, `moltis-media`, `moltis-routing`, `moltis-canvas`, `moltis-auto-reply`, `moltis-schema-export`, `moltis-benchmarks` | 2.5K | 107→ 108→Use `--no-default-features --features lightweight` for constrained devices (Raspberry Pi, etc.). 109→ 110→## Security 111→ 112→- **Zero `unsafe` code\*** — denied workspace-wide; only opt-in FFI behind `local-embeddings` flag 113→- **Sandboxed execution** — Docker + Apple Container, per-session isolation 114→- **Secret handling** — `secrecy::Secret`, zeroed on drop, redacted from tool output 115→- **Authentication** — password + passkey (WebAuthn), rate-limited, per-IP throttle 116→- **SSRF protection** — DNS-resolved, blocks loopback/private/link-local 117→- **Origin validation** — rejects cross-origin WebSocket upgrades 118→- **Hook gating** — `BeforeToolCall` hooks can inspect/block any tool invocation 119→ 120→See [Security Architecture](https://docs.moltis.org/security.html) for details. 121→ 122→## Features 123→ 124→- **AI Gateway** — Multi-provider LLM support (OpenAI Codex, GitHub Copilot, Local), streaming responses, agent loop with sub-agent delegation, parallel tool execution 125→- **Communication** — Web UI, Telegram, Microsoft Teams, Discord, API access, voice I/O (8 TTS + 7 STT providers), mobile PWA with push notifications 126→- **Memory & Recall** — Per-agent memory workspaces, embeddings-powered long-term memory, hybrid vector + full-text search, session persistence with auto-compaction, cross-session recall, Cursor-compatible project context, context-file safety scanning 127→- **Safer Agent Editing** — Automatic checkpoints before built-in skill and memory mutations, restore tooling, session branching 128→- **Extensibility** — MCP servers (stdio + HTTP/SSE), skill system, 15 lifecycle hook events with circuit breaker, destructive command guard 129→- **Security** — Encryption-at-rest vault (XChaCha20-Poly1305 + Argon2id), password + passkey + API key auth, sandbox isolation, SSRF/CSWSH protection 130→- **Operations** — Cron scheduling, OpenTelemetry tracing, Prometheus metrics, cloud deploy (Fly.io, DigitalOcean), Tailscale integration, managed SSH deploy keys, host-pinned remote targets, live tool inventory in Settings, and CLI/web remote-exec doctor flows 131→ 132→## How It Works 133→ 134→Moltis is a **local-first persistent agent server** — a single Rust binary that 135→sits between you and multiple LLM providers, keeps durable session state, and 136→can meet you across channels without handing your data to a cloud relay. 137→ 138→``` 139→┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ 140→│ Web UI │ │ Telegram │ │ Discord │ 141→└──────┬──────┘ └──────┬──────┘ └──────┬──────┘ 142→ │ │ │ 143→ └────────┬───────┴────────┬───────┘ 144→ │ WebSocket │ 145→ ▼ ▼ 146→ ┌─────────────────────────────────┐ 147→ │ Gateway Server │ 148→ │ (Axum · HTTP · WS · Auth) │ 149→ ├─────────────────────────────────┤ 150→ │ Chat Service │ 151→ │ ┌───────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ │ 152→ │ │ Agent │ │ Tool │ │ 153→ │ │ Runner │◄┤ Registry │ │ 154→ │ └─────┬─────┘ └─────────────┘ │ 155→ │ │ │ 156→ │ ┌─────▼─────────────────────┐ │ 157→ │ │ Provider Registry │ │ 158→ │ │ Multiple providers │ │ 159→ │ │ (Codex · Copilot · Local)│ │ 160→ │ └───────────────────────────┘ │ 161→ ├─────────────────────────────────┤ 162→ │ Sessions │ Memory │ Hooks │ 163→ │ (JSONL) │ (SQLite)│ (events) │ 164→ └─────────────────────────────────┘ 165→ │ 166→ ┌───────▼───────┐ 167→ │ Sandbox │ 168→ │ Docker/Apple │ 169→ │ Container │ 170→ └───────────────┘ 171→``` 172→ 173→See [Quickstart](https://docs.moltis.org/quickstart.html) for gateway startup, message flow, sessions, and memory details. 174→ 175→## Getting Started 176→ 177→### Build & Run 178→ 179→Requires [just](https://github.com/casey/just) (command runner) and Node.js (for Tailwind CSS). 180→ 181→```bash 182→git clone https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis.git 183→cd moltis 184→just build-css # Build Tailwind CSS for the web UI 185→just build-release # Build in release mode 186→cargo run --release --bin moltis 187→``` 188→ 189→For a full release build including WASM sandbox tools: 190→ 191→```bash 192→just build-release-with-wasm # Builds WASM artifacts + release binary 193→cargo run --release --bin moltis 194→``` 195→ 196→Open `https://moltis.localhost:3000`. On first run, a setup code is printed to 197→the terminal — enter it in the web UI to set your password or register a passkey. 198→ 199→Optional flags: `--config-dir /path/to/config --data-dir /path/to/data` 200→ 201→### Docker 202→ 203→```bash 204→# Docker / OrbStack 205→docker run -d \ 206→ --name moltis \ 207→ -p 13131:13131 \ 208→ -p 13132:13132 \ 209→ -p 1455:1455 \ 210→ -v moltis-config:/home/moltis/.config/moltis \ 211→ -v moltis-data:/home/moltis/.moltis \ 212→ -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \ 213→ ghcr.io/moltis-org/moltis:latest 214→``` 215→ 216→Open `https://localhost:13131` and complete the setup. For unattended Docker 217→deployments, set `MOLTIS_PASSWORD`, `MOLTIS_PROVIDER`, and `MOLTIS_API_KEY` 218→before first boot to skip the setup wizard. See [Docker docs](https://docs.moltis.org/docker.html) 219→for Podman, OrbStack, TLS trust, and persistence details. 220→ 221→### Cloud Deployment 222→ 223→| Provider | Deploy | 224→|----------|--------| 225→| DigitalOcean | [![Deploy to DO](https://www.deploytodo.com/do-btn-blue.svg)](https://cloud.digitalocean.com/apps/new?repo=https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/tree/main) | 226→ 227→**Fly.io** (CLI): 228→ 229→```bash 230→fly launch --image ghcr.io/moltis-org/moltis:latest 231→fly secrets set MOLTIS_PASSWORD="your-password" 232→``` 233→ 234→All cloud configs use `--no-tls` because the provider handles TLS termination. 235→See [Cloud Deploy docs](https://docs.moltis.org/cloud-deploy.html) for details. 236→ 237→## Star History 238→ 239→[![Star History Chart](https://api.star-history.com/svg?repos=moltis-org/moltis&type=date&legend=top-left)](https://www.star-history.com/#moltis-org/moltis&type=date&legend=top-left) 240→ 241→## License 242→ 243→MIT 244→

1→# Security Architecture
     2→
     3→Moltis is designed with a defense-in-depth security model. This document
     4→explains the key security features and provides guidance for production
     5→deployments.
     6→
     7→## Overview
     8→
     9→Moltis runs AI agents that can execute code and interact with external systems.
    10→This power requires multiple layers of protection:
    11→
    12→1. **Human-in-the-loop approval** for dangerous commands
    13→2. **Sandbox isolation** for command execution
    14→3. **Channel authorization** for external integrations
    15→4. **Rate limiting** to prevent resource abuse
    16→5. **Scope-based access control** for API authorization
    17→
    18→For marketplace-style skill/plugin hardening (trust gating, provenance pinning,
    19→drift re-trust, dependency install guards, kill switch, audit log), see
    20→[Third-Party Skills Security](skills-security.md).
    21→
    22→## Command Execution Approval
    23→
    24→By default, Moltis requires explicit user approval before executing potentially
    25→dangerous commands. This "human-in-the-loop" design ensures the AI cannot take
    26→destructive actions without consent.
    27→
    28→### How It Works
    29→
    30→When the agent wants to run a command:
    31→
    32→1. The command is analyzed against approval policies
    33→2. If approval is required, the user sees a prompt in the UI
    34→3. The user can approve, deny, or modify the command
    35→4. Only approved commands execute
    36→
    37→### Approval Policies
    38→
    39→Configure approval behavior in `moltis.toml`:
    40→
    41→```toml
    42→[tools.exec]
    43→approval_mode = "always"  # always require approval
    44→# approval_mode = "smart" # auto-approve safe commands (default)
    45→# approval_mode = "never" # dangerous: never require approval
    46→```
    47→
    48→**Recommendation**: Keep `approval_mode = "smart"` (the default) for most use
    49→cases. Only use `"never"` in fully automated, sandboxed environments.
    50→
    51→### Built-in Dangerous Command Blocklist
    52→
    53→Even with `approval_mode = "never"` or `security_level = "full"`, Moltis
    54→maintains a safety floor: a hardcoded set of regex patterns for the most
    55→critical destructive commands (e.g. `rm -rf /`, `git reset --hard`,
    56→`DROP TABLE`, `mkfs`, `terraform destroy`). Matching commands always require
    57→approval regardless of configuration.
    58→
    59→Users can override specific patterns by adding matching entries to their
    60→`allowlist` in `moltis.toml`. The blocklist only applies to host execution;
    61→sandboxed commands are already isolated.
    62→
    63→### Destructive Command Guard (dcg)
    64→
    65→For broader coverage beyond the built-in blocklist, install the
    66→[Destructive Command Guard](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard)
    67→(dcg) as a hook. dcg adds 49+ pattern categories including heredoc/inline-script
    68→scanning, database, cloud, and infrastructure patterns.
    69→
    70→See [Hooks: Destructive Command Guard](hooks.md#recommended-destructive-command-guard-dcg)
    71→for setup instructions.
    72→
    73→dcg complements (does not replace) sandbox isolation and the approval system.
    74→
    75→## Sandbox Isolation
    76→
    77→Commands execute inside isolated containers (Docker or Apple Container) by
    78→default. This protects your host system from:
    79→
    80→- Accidental file deletion or modification
    81→- Malicious code execution
    82→- Resource exhaustion (memory, CPU, disk)
    83→
    84→See [sandbox.md](sandbox.md) for backend configuration.
    85→
    86→### Resource Limits
    87→
    88→```toml
    89→[tools.exec.sandbox.resource_limits]
    90→memory_limit = "512M"
    91→cpu_quota = 1.0
    92→pids_max = 256
    93→```
    94→
    95→### Network Isolation
    96→
    97→Sandbox containers have no network access by default (`no_network = true`).
    98→
    99→For tasks that need internet access, [trusted network mode](trusted-network.md)
   100→provides a proxy-filtered allowlist — only connections to explicitly approved
   101→domains are permitted. All requests (allowed and denied) are recorded in the
   102→network audit log for review.
   103→
   104→## Channel Authorization
   105→
   106→Channels (Telegram, Slack, etc.) allow external parties to interact with your
   107→Moltis agent. This requires careful access control.
   108→
   109→### Sender Allowlisting
   110→
   111→When a new sender contacts the agent through a channel, they are placed in a
   112→pending queue. You must explicitly approve or deny each sender before they can
   113→interact with the agent.
   114→
   115→```
   116→UI: Settings > Channels > Pending Senders
   117→```
   118→
   119→### Per-Channel Permissions
   120→
   121→Each channel can have different permission levels:
   122→
   123→- **Read-only**: Sender can ask questions, agent responds
   124→- **Execute**: Sender can trigger actions (with approval still required)
   125→- **Admin**: Full access including configuration changes
   126→
   127→### Channel Isolation
   128→
   129→Channels run in isolated sessions by default. A malicious message from one
   130→channel cannot affect another channel's session or the main UI session.
   131→
   132→## Cron Job Security
   133→
   134→Scheduled tasks (cron jobs) can run agent turns automatically. Security
   135→considerations:
   136→
   137→### Rate Limiting
   138→
   139→To prevent prompt injection attacks from rapidly creating many cron jobs:
   140→
   141→```toml
   142→[cron]
   143→rate_limit_max = 10           # max jobs per window
   144→rate_limit_window_secs = 60   # window duration (1 minute)
   145→```
   146→
   147→This limits job creation to 10 per minute by default. System jobs (like
   148→heartbeat) bypass this limit.
   149→
   150→### Job Notifications
   151→
   152→When cron jobs are created, updated, or removed, Moltis broadcasts events:
   153→
   154→- `cron.job.created` - A new job was created
   155→- `cron.job.updated` - An existing job was modified
   156→- `cron.job.removed` - A job was deleted
   157→
   158→Monitor these events to detect suspicious automated job creation.
   159→
   160→### Sandbox for Cron Jobs
   161→
   162→Cron job execution uses sandbox isolation by default:
   163→
   164→```toml
   165→# Per-job configuration
   166→[cron.job.sandbox]
   167→enabled = true              # run in sandbox (default)
   168→# image = "custom:latest"   # optional custom image
   169→```
   170→
   171→## Identity Protection
   172→
   173→The agent's identity fields (name, emoji, creature, vibe) are stored in `IDENTITY.md`
   174→YAML frontmatter at the workspace root (`data_dir`).
   175→User profile fields are stored in `USER.md` YAML frontmatter at the same location.
   176→The personality text is stored separately in `SOUL.md` at the workspace root (`data_dir`).
   177→Tool guidance is stored in `TOOLS.md` at the workspace root (`data_dir`) and is injected
   178→as workspace context in the system prompt.
   179→Modifying identity requires the `operator.write` scope, not just `operator.read`.
   180→
   181→This prevents prompt injection attacks from subtly modifying the agent's
   182→personality to make it more compliant with malicious requests.
   183→
   184→## API Authorization
   185→
   186→The gateway API uses role-based access control with scopes:
   187→
   188→| Scope | Permissions |
   189→|-------|-------------|
   190→| `operator.read` | View status, list jobs, read history |
   191→| `operator.write` | Send messages, create jobs, modify configuration |
   192→| `operator.admin` | All permissions (includes all other scopes) |
   193→| `operator.approvals` | Handle command approval requests |
   194→| `operator.pairing` | Manage device/node pairing |
   195→
   196→### API Keys
   197→
   198→API keys authenticate external tools and scripts connecting to Moltis. Keys
   199→**must specify at least one scope** — keys without scopes are denied access
   200→(least-privilege by default).
   201→
   202→#### Creating API Keys
   203→
   204→**Web UI**: Settings > Security > API Keys
   205→
   206→1. Enter a label describing the key's purpose
   207→2. Select the required scopes
   208→3. Click "Generate key"
   209→4. **Copy the key immediately** — it's only shown once
   210→
   211→**CLI**:
   212→
   213→```bash
   214→# Scoped key (comma-separated scopes)
   215→moltis auth create-api-key --label "Monitor" --scopes "operator.read"
   216→moltis auth create-api-key --label "Automation" --scopes "operator.read,operator.write"
   217→moltis auth create-api-key --label "CI pipeline" --scopes "operator.admin"
   218→```
   219→
   220→#### Using API Keys
   221→
   222→Pass the key in the `connect` handshake over WebSocket:
   223→
   224→```json
   225→{
   226→  "method": "connect",
   227→  "params": {
   228→    "client": { "id": "my-tool", "version": "1.0.0" },
   229→    "auth": { "api_key": "mk_abc123..." }
   230→  }
   231→}
   232→```
   233→
   234→Or use Bearer authentication for REST API calls:
   235→
   236→```
   237→Authorization: Bearer mk_abc123...
   238→```
   239→
   240→#### Scope Recommendations
   241→
   242→| Use Case | Recommended Scopes |
   243→|----------|-------------------|
   244→| Read-only monitoring | `operator.read` |
   245→| Automated workflows | `operator.read`, `operator.write` |
   246→| Approval handling | `operator.read`, `operator.approvals` |
   247→| Full automation | `operator.admin` |
   248→
   249→**Best practice**: Use the minimum necessary scopes. If a key only needs to
   250→read status and logs, don't grant `operator.write`.
   251→
   252→#### Backward Compatibility
   253→
   254→Existing API keys created without scopes will be **denied access** until
   255→scopes are added. Re-create keys with explicit scopes to restore access.
   256→
   257→## Encryption at Rest
   258→
   259→Sensitive data in the SQLite database (environment variables containing
   260→API keys, tokens, etc.) is encrypted at rest using XChaCha20-Poly1305.
   261→The encryption key is derived from the user's password via Argon2id.
   262→
   263→The vault initializes when a first password is set (during setup or later
   264→in Settings > Authentication), unseals automatically on login, and
   265→re-seals on server restart. A recovery key is provided at initialization
   266→for emergency access.
   267→
   268→When the vault is sealed, a middleware layer blocks vault-protected API
   269→requests with `423 Locked`. Session history and bootstrap endpoints remain
   270→available because those payloads are not yet encrypted at rest.
   271→
   272→For full details on the key hierarchy, vault states, API endpoints, and
   273→cryptographic parameters, see [Encryption at Rest (Vault)](vault.md).
   274→
   275→## Network Security
   276→
   277→### TLS Encryption
   278→
   279→HTTPS is enabled by default with auto-generated certificates:
   280→
   281→```toml
   282→[tls]
   283→enabled = true
   284→auto_generate = true
   285→```
   286→
   287→For production, use certificates from a trusted CA or configure custom
   288→certificates.
   289→
   290→### Origin Validation
   291→
   292→WebSocket connections validate the `Origin` header to prevent cross-site
   293→WebSocket hijacking (CSWSH). Connections from untrusted origins are rejected.
   294→
   295→### SSRF Protection
   296→
   297→The `web_fetch` tool resolves DNS and blocks requests to private IP ranges
   298→(loopback, RFC 1918, link-local, CGNAT). This prevents server-side request
   299→forgery attacks.
   300→
   301→To allow access to trusted private networks (e.g. Docker sibling containers),
   302→add their CIDR ranges to `ssrf_allowlist`:
   303→
   304→```toml
   305→[tools.web.fetch]
   306→ssrf_allowlist = ["172.22.0.0/16"]
   307→```
   308→
   309→**Warning:** Only add networks you trust. The allowlist bypasses SSRF protection
   310→for the listed ranges. Never add cloud metadata ranges (`169.254.169.254/32`)
   311→unless you understand the risk.
   312→
   313→## Authentication
   314→
   315→Moltis uses a unified auth gate that applies a single `check_auth()`
   316→function to every request. This prevents split-brain bugs where different
   317→code paths disagree on auth status.
   318→
   319→For full details — including the decision matrix, credential types, API
   320→key scopes, session management endpoints, and WebSocket auth — see the
   321→dedicated [Authentication](authentication.md) page.
   322→
   323→### Three-Tier Model (summary)
   324→
   325→| Tier | Condition | Behaviour |
   326→|------|-----------|-----------|
   327→| **1** | Password/passkey is configured | Auth **always** required (any IP) |
   328→| **2** | No credentials + direct local connection | Full access (dev convenience) |
   329→| **3** | No credentials + remote/proxied connection | Onboarding only (setup code required) |
   330→
   331→## HTTP Endpoint Throttling
   332→
   333→Moltis includes built-in per-IP endpoint throttling to reduce brute force
   334→attempts and traffic spikes, but only when auth is required for the current
   335→request.
   336→
   337→Throttling is bypassed when a request is already authenticated, when auth is
   338→explicitly disabled, or when setup is incomplete and local Tier-2 access is
   339→allowed.
   340→
   341→### Default Limits
   342→
   343→| Scope | Default |
   344→|------|---------|
   345→| `POST /api/auth/login` | 5 requests per 60 seconds |
   346→| Other `/api/auth/*` | 120 requests per 60 seconds |
   347→| Other `/api/*` | 180 requests per 60 seconds |
   348→| `/ws/chat` upgrade | 30 requests per 60 seconds |
   349→| `/ws` upgrade | 30 requests per 60 seconds |
   350→
   351→### When Limits Are Hit
   352→
   353→- API endpoints return `429 Too Many Requests`
   354→- Responses include `Retry-After`
   355→- JSON responses include `retry_after_seconds`
   356→
   357→### Reverse Proxy Behavior
   358→
   359→When `MOLTIS_BEHIND_PROXY=true`, throttling is keyed by forwarded client IP
   360→headers (`X-Forwarded-For`, `X-Real-IP`, `CF-Connecting-IP`) instead of the
   361→direct socket address.
   362→
   363→### Production Guidance
   364→
   365→Built-in throttling is the first layer. For internet-facing deployments, add
   366→edge rate limits at your reverse proxy or WAF as a second layer (IP reputation,
   367→burst controls, geo rules, bot filtering).
   368→
   369→## Reverse Proxy Deployments
   370→
   371→Running Moltis behind a reverse proxy (Caddy, nginx, Traefik, etc.)
   372→requires understanding how authentication interacts with loopback
   373→connections.
   374→
   375→### The problem
   376→
   377→When Moltis binds to `127.0.0.1` and a proxy on the same machine
   378→forwards traffic to it, **every** incoming TCP connection appears to
   379→originate from `127.0.0.1` — including requests from the public
   380→internet.  A naive "trust all loopback connections" check would bypass
   381→authentication for all proxied traffic.
   382→
   383→This is the same class of vulnerability as
   384→[CVE-2026-25253](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/security/advisories/GHSA-g8p2-7wf7-98mq),
   385→which allowed one-click remote code execution on OpenClaw through
   386→authentication token exfiltration and cross-site WebSocket hijacking.
   387→
   388→### How Moltis handles it
   389→
   390→Moltis uses the per-request `is_local_connection()` check described
   391→above.  Most reverse proxies add forwarding headers or change the
   392→`Host` header, which automatically triggers the "remote" classification.
   393→
   394→For proxies that **strip all signals** (e.g. a bare nginx `proxy_pass`
   395→that rewrites `Host` to the upstream address and adds no `X-Forwarded-For`),
   396→use the `MOLTIS_BEHIND_PROXY` environment variable as a hard override:
   397→
   398→```bash
   399→MOLTIS_BEHIND_PROXY=true moltis
   400→```
   401→
   402→When this variable is set, **all** connections are treated as remote —
   403→no loopback bypass, no exceptions.
   404→
   405→### Deploying behind a proxy
   406→
   407→1. **Set `MOLTIS_BEHIND_PROXY=true`** if your proxy does not add
   408→   forwarding headers (safest option — eliminates any ambiguity).
   409→
   410→2. **Set a password or register a passkey** during initial setup.
   411→   Once a password is configured (Tier 1), authentication is required
   412→   for all traffic regardless of `is_local_connection()`.
   413→
   414→3. **WebSocket proxying** must preserve browser origin host info
   415→   (`Host`, or `X-Forwarded-Host` if `Host` is rewritten). Moltis
   416→   validates same-origin on WebSocket upgrades to prevent cross-site
   417→   WebSocket hijacking (CSWSH).
   418→
   419→4. **TLS termination** should happen at the proxy. Run Moltis with
   420→   `--no-tls` (or `MOLTIS_NO_TLS=true`) in this mode.
   421→
   422→   If your browser is being redirected to `https://<domain>:13131`,
   423→   Moltis TLS is still enabled while your proxy upstream is plain HTTP.
   424→
   425→5. **Advanced TLS upstream mode** (optional): if your proxy connects to
   426→   Moltis using HTTPS upstream (or TCP TLS passthrough), you may keep
   427→   Moltis TLS enabled. Set `MOLTIS_ALLOW_TLS_BEHIND_PROXY=true` to
   428→   acknowledge this non-default setup.
   429→
   430→### Nginx (direct config example)
   431→
   432→If HTTP works but WebSockets fail, make sure your location block includes
   433→`proxy_http_version 1.1;` and upgrade headers.
   434→
   435→```nginx
   436→location / {
   437→    proxy_pass http://172.17.0.1:13131;
   438→    proxy_set_header Host $host;
   439→    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Host $host;
   440→    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
   441→    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
   442→    proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
   443→
   444→    # WebSocket upgrade support
   445→    proxy_http_version 1.1;
   446→    proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
   447→    proxy_set_header Connection $connection_upgrade;
   448→}
   449→```
   450→
   451→If you use `$connection_upgrade`, define it once in the `http {}` block:
   452→
   453→```nginx
   454→map $http_upgrade $connection_upgrade {
   455→    default upgrade;
   456→    ''      close;
   457→}
   458→```
   459→
   460→### Nginx Proxy Manager (known-good headers)
   461→
   462→If WebSockets fail behind NPM while HTTP works, ensure:
   463→
   464→- Moltis runs with `MOLTIS_BEHIND_PROXY=true`
   465→- For standard edge TLS termination, Moltis runs with `--no-tls`
   466→- NPM preserves browser host/origin context
   467→
   468→Use this in NPM's **Advanced** field:
   469→
   470→```nginx
   471→proxy_set_header Host $host;
   472→proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Host $host;
   473→proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
   474→proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
   475→proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
   476→proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
   477→```
   478→
   479→Upstream scheme guidance:
   480→
   481→- **Edge TLS termination (most setups)**: proxy to `http://<moltis-host>:13131`
   482→  with Moltis started using `--no-tls`
   483→- **HTTPS upstream / TLS passthrough**: proxy to `https://<moltis-host>:13131`
   484→  and set `MOLTIS_ALLOW_TLS_BEHIND_PROXY=true`
   485→
   486→### Passkeys Behind Proxies (Host Changes)
   487→
   488→WebAuthn passkeys are bound to an RP ID (domain identity), not just the
   489→server process. In practice:
   490→
   491→- If users move from one hostname to another, old passkeys for the old host
   492→  will not authenticate on the new host.
   493→- If a proxy rewrites `Host` and does not preserve browser host context,
   494→  passkey routes can fail with "no passkey config for this hostname".
   495→
   496→For stable proxy deployments, set explicit WebAuthn identity to your public
   497→domain:
   498→
   499→```bash
   500→MOLTIS_BEHIND_PROXY=true
   501→MOLTIS_NO_TLS=true
   502→MOLTIS_WEBAUTHN_RP_ID=chat.example.com
   503→MOLTIS_WEBAUTHN_ORIGIN=https://chat.example.com
   504→```
   505→
   506→Migration guidance when changing host/domain:
   507→
   508→1. Keep password login enabled during migration.
   509→2. Deploy with the new `MOLTIS_WEBAUTHN_RP_ID`/`MOLTIS_WEBAUTHN_ORIGIN`.
   510→3. Ask users to register a new passkey on the new host.
   511→4. Remove old passkeys after new-host login is confirmed.
   512→
   513→## Production Recommendations
   514→
   515→### 1. Enable Authentication
   516→
   517→By default, Moltis requires a password when accessed from non-localhost:
   518→
   519→```toml
   520→[auth]
   521→disabled = false  # keep this false in production
   522→```
   523→
   524→### 2. Use Sandbox Isolation
   525→
   526→Always run with sandbox enabled in production:
   527→
   528→```toml
   529→[tools.exec.sandbox]
   530→enabled = true
   531→backend = "auto"  # uses strongest available
   532→```
   533→
   534→### 3. Limit Rate Limits
   535→
   536→Tighten rate limits for untrusted environments:
   537→
   538→```toml
   539→[cron]
   540→rate_limit_max = 5
   541→rate_limit_window_secs = 300  # 5 per 5 minutes
   542→```
   543→
   544→### 4. Review Channel Senders
   545→
   546→Regularly audit approved senders and revoke access for unknown parties.
   547→
   548→### 5. Monitor Events
   549→
   550→Watch for these suspicious patterns:
   551→
   552→- Rapid cron job creation
   553→- Identity modification attempts
   554→- Unusual command patterns in approval requests
   555→- New channel senders from unexpected sources
   556→
   557→### 6. Network Segmentation
   558→
   559→Run Moltis on a private network or behind a reverse proxy with:
   560→
   561→- IP allowlisting
   562→- Rate limiting
   563→- Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules
   564→
   565→### 7. Keep Software Updated
   566→
   567→Subscribe to security advisories and update promptly when vulnerabilities are
   568→disclosed.
   569→
   570→## Release Signing and Verification
   571→
   572→All release artifacts are signed with two independent methods:
   573→
   574→1. **Sigstore keyless signing** (automated in CI) — proves the artifact was
   575→   built by the `moltis-org/moltis` GitHub Actions pipeline
   576→2. **GPG signing** (maintainer's YubiKey hardware key) — proves a specific
   577→   maintainer authorized the release
   578→
   579→Checksums (SHA-256 and SHA-512) are generated for every artifact.
   580→
   581→See [Release Verification](release-verification.md) for detailed verification
   582→instructions, artifact file extensions, and maintainer signing workflow.
   583→
   584→## Reporting Security Issues
   585→
   586→Report security vulnerabilities privately to the maintainers. Do not open
   587→public issues for security bugs.
   588→
   589→See the repository's SECURITY.md for contact information.
   590→

1→# Release Verification
     2→
     3→Moltis releases use **dual signing** to provide strong supply chain guarantees:
     4→
     5→| Method | Proves | Verification |
     6→|--------|--------|-------------|
     7→| **Sigstore** (keyless, CI-generated) | Artifact was built by GitHub Actions from this repo | `cosign verify-blob` |
     8→| **GPG** (YubiKey-resident key, maintainer-signed) | A specific maintainer authorized the release | `gpg --verify` |
     9→| **SHA256/SHA512 checksums** | File integrity (no corruption/tampering in transit) | `sha256sum --check` |
    10→
    11→## Quick Verification
    12→
    13→The easiest way to verify a release is with the included script:
    14→
    15→```bash
    16→# Verify all artifacts for a release (fetches GPG key automatically)
    17→./scripts/verify-release.sh --version VERSION
    18→
    19→# Also check SHA256 checksums
    20→./scripts/verify-release.sh --checksums --version VERSION
    21→
    22→# Verify specific local files
    23→./scripts/verify-release.sh moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz
    24→```
    25→
    26→### Manual Verification
    27→
    28→#### 1. Verify checksums
    29→
    30→```bash
    31→# Download the artifact and its checksum
    32→curl -LO https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/releases/download/VERSION/moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz
    33→curl -LO https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/releases/download/VERSION/moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.sha256
    34→
    35→sha256sum --check moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.sha256
    36→```
    37→
    38→#### 2. Verify GPG signature
    39→
    40→The maintainer's GPG key fingerprint is:
    41→
    42→```
    43→3103 20A8 CC1C 5BA8 6AD0  9040 C045 1BAD F764 9BBF
    44→```
    45→
    46→```bash
    47→# Import the maintainer's public key (one-time)
    48→curl -fsSL https://pen.so/gpg.asc | gpg --import
    49→
    50→# Confirm the fingerprint matches
    51→gpg --fingerprint F7649BBF
    52→
    53→# Download the detached signature
    54→curl -LO https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/releases/download/VERSION/moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.asc
    55→
    56→# Verify
    57→gpg --verify \
    58→  moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.asc \
    59→  moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz
    60→```
    61→
    62→You should see `Good signature from ...` with the maintainer's identity.
    63→
    64→#### 3. Verify Sigstore signature
    65→
    66→```bash
    67→# Install cosign: https://docs.sigstore.dev/cosign/system_config/installation/
    68→
    69→# Download signature and certificate
    70→curl -LO https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/releases/download/VERSION/moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.sig
    71→curl -LO https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/releases/download/VERSION/moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.crt
    72→
    73→cosign verify-blob \
    74→  --signature moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.sig \
    75→  --certificate moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.crt \
    76→  --certificate-oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com \
    77→  --certificate-identity-regexp 'https://github\.com/moltis-org/moltis/' \
    78→  moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz
    79→```
    80→
    81→#### 4. Verify Docker images
    82→
    83→```bash
    84→cosign verify \
    85→  --certificate-oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com \
    86→  --certificate-identity-regexp 'https://github\.com/moltis-org/moltis/' \
    87→  ghcr.io/moltis-org/moltis:VERSION
    88→```
    89→
    90→## What Each Layer Proves
    91→
    92→**Checksums** detect download corruption or CDN tampering. They do not prove
    93→who created the file.
    94→
    95→**Sigstore signatures** prove the artifact was built inside the
    96→`moltis-org/moltis` GitHub Actions workflow using OIDC-based keyless signing.
    97→This guards against a compromised maintainer laptop — even if someone steals
    98→credentials, they cannot reproduce a valid Sigstore certificate from the CI
    99→environment.
   100→
   101→**GPG signatures** prove the release was reviewed and authorized by a specific
   102→maintainer holding the corresponding private key. Because the key lives on a
   103→YubiKey hardware token, compromise requires physical access to the device plus
   104→the PIN.
   105→
   106→Together, the two signatures create a strong chain: Sigstore proves *where* the
   107→artifact was built (CI), and GPG proves *who* authorized it (maintainer with
   108→hardware key).
   109→
   110→## Release Artifacts Per File
   111→
   112→Each release artifact (`.deb`, `.rpm`, `.tar.gz`, `.exe`, etc.) may have:
   113→
   114→| Extension | Source | Description |
   115→|-----------|--------|-------------|
   116→| `.sha256` | CI | SHA-256 checksum |
   117→| `.sha512` | CI | SHA-512 checksum |
   118→| `.sig` | CI | Sigstore detached signature |
   119→| `.crt` | CI | Sigstore signing certificate |
   120→| `.asc` | Maintainer | GPG detached armored signature |
   121→
   122→## For Maintainers: Signing a Release
   123→
   124→After CI publishes a release, sign the artifacts locally using your
   125→YubiKey-resident GPG key:
   126→
   127→```bash
   128→# Sign the latest release (prompts for YubiKey PIN/touch)
   129→./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh
   130→
   131→# Sign a specific version
   132→./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh 20260331.01
   133→
   134→# Use a specific key
   135→./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh --key 0xABCD1234 20260331.01
   136→
   137→# Dry run (sign locally, don't upload)
   138→./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh --dry-run 20260331.01
   139→```
   140→
   141→The script:
   142→1. Downloads all release packages from GitHub
   143→2. Verifies their SHA256 checksums against CI-generated values
   144→3. Creates `.asc` detached GPG signatures for each artifact
   145→4. Uploads the `.asc` files to the GitHub release
   146→
   147→### Prerequisites
   148→
   149→- [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/) (`gh`) authenticated
   150→- GPG with your signing key available (YubiKey inserted)
   151→- The `GPG_KEY_ID` environment variable or `--key` flag if you have
   152→  multiple secret keys
   153→
   154→### Publish Your Public Key
   155→
   156→Upload your public GPG key so users can verify signatures:
   157→
   158→```bash
   159→# Export and upload to a keyserver
   160→gpg --export --armor YOUR_KEY_ID | \
   161→  curl -T - https://keys.openpgp.org
   162→
   163→# Also add to your GitHub profile:
   164→# Settings > SSH and GPG keys > New GPG key
   165→gpg --export --armor YOUR_KEY_ID
   166→```
   167→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→
     3→set -euo pipefail
     4→
     5→# GPG-sign release artifacts using a local key (e.g. YubiKey-resident).
     6→#
     7→# This script downloads release artifacts from a GitHub release, creates
     8→# detached GPG signatures (.asc), and uploads them back to the release.
     9→# It complements the CI-generated Sigstore signatures with a personal
    10→# GPG signature tied to the release maintainer's identity.
    11→#
    12→# Prerequisites:
    13→#   - gh (GitHub CLI) authenticated with repo access
    14→#   - gpg with your signing key available (e.g. YubiKey plugged in)
    15→#
    16→# Usage:
    17→#   ./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh [VERSION]
    18→#   ./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh 20260331.01
    19→#   ./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh              # signs the latest release
    20→
    21→usage() {
    22→  cat <<'EOF'
    23→Usage: ./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh [VERSION]
    24→
    25→Signs release artifacts with a local GPG key (e.g. YubiKey-resident).
    26→
    27→  VERSION   Tag to sign (e.g. 20260331.01). Defaults to the latest release.
    28→
    29→Options:
    30→  -k, --key KEY_ID    GPG key ID or fingerprint to sign with
    31→  -n, --dry-run       Download and sign but do not upload .asc files
    32→  -y, --yes           Skip confirmation prompt
    33→  -h, --help          Show this help
    34→
    35→Environment:
    36→  GPG_KEY_ID          Default GPG key (overridden by --key)
    37→  MOLTIS_REPO         GitHub repo (default: moltis-org/moltis)
    38→
    39→Examples:
    40→  ./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh                          # latest, default key
    41→  ./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh -k 0xABCD1234 20260331.01
    42→  GPG_KEY_ID=0xABCD1234 ./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh
    43→EOF
    44→}
    45→
    46→# --- Parse arguments ---
    47→KEY_ID="${GPG_KEY_ID:-}"
    48→REPO="${MOLTIS_REPO:-moltis-org/moltis}"
    49→DRY_RUN=false
    50→SKIP_CONFIRM=false
    51→VERSION=""
    52→
    53→while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    54→  case "$1" in
    55→    -k|--key)    KEY_ID="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    56→    -n|--dry-run) DRY_RUN=true; shift ;;
    57→    -y|--yes)    SKIP_CONFIRM=true; shift ;;
    58→    -h|--help)   usage; exit 0 ;;
    59→    -*)          echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; usage; exit 1 ;;
    60→    *)           VERSION="$1"; shift ;;
    61→  esac
    62→done
    63→
    64→# --- Preflight checks ---
    65→for cmd in gh gpg; do
    66→  if ! command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    67→    echo "error: $cmd is required but not found" >&2
    68→    exit 1
    69→  fi
    70→done
    71→
    72→# Resolve version
    73→if [[ -z "$VERSION" ]]; then
    74→  VERSION="$(gh release view --repo "$REPO" --json tagName --jq '.tagName')"
    75→  echo "Resolved latest release: $VERSION"
    76→fi
    77→
    78→# Validate tag format
    79→if [[ ! "$VERSION" =~ ^[0-9]{8}\.[0-9]{1,2}$ ]]; then
    80→  echo "error: '$VERSION' does not match YYYYMMDD.NN format" >&2
    81→  exit 1
    82→fi
    83→
    84→# Verify the release exists
    85→if ! gh release view "$VERSION" --repo "$REPO" --json tagName >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    86→  echo "error: release '$VERSION' not found in $REPO" >&2
    87→  exit 1
    88→fi
    89→
    90→# Verify GPG key is available
    91→GPG_SIGN_ARGS=()
    92→if [[ -n "$KEY_ID" ]]; then
    93→  GPG_SIGN_ARGS+=(--local-user "$KEY_ID")
    94→  echo "Using GPG key: $KEY_ID"
    95→else
    96→  # Let GPG use its default key; verify one exists
    97→  DEFAULT_KEY="$(gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format long 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)"
    98→  if [[ -z "$DEFAULT_KEY" ]]; then
    99→    echo "error: no GPG secret key found. Specify --key or set GPG_KEY_ID" >&2
   100→    exit 1
   101→  fi
   102→  echo "Using default GPG signing key"
   103→fi
   104→
   105→# Quick liveness check — attempt a throwaway signature to confirm the key
   106→# is accessible (prompts for YubiKey PIN/touch if needed early).
   107→PROBE="$(mktemp)"
   108→echo "gpg-sign-release probe" > "$PROBE"
   109→if ! gpg --batch "${GPG_SIGN_ARGS[@]}" --armor --detach-sign "$PROBE" 2>/dev/null; then
   110→  rm -f "$PROBE" "${PROBE}.asc"
   111→  echo "error: GPG signing failed. Is your YubiKey inserted and unlocked?" >&2
   112→  exit 1
   113→fi
   114→rm -f "$PROBE" "${PROBE}.asc"
   115→echo "GPG key verified (signing probe succeeded)"
   116→
   117→# --- Download release artifacts ---
   118→WORK_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
   119→trap 'rm -rf "$WORK_DIR"' EXIT
   120→
   121→echo ""
   122→echo "Downloading release artifacts for $VERSION..."
   123→gh release download "$VERSION" \
   124→  --repo "$REPO" \
   125→  --dir "$WORK_DIR" \
   126→  --pattern '*.deb' \
   127→  --pattern '*.rpm' \
   128→  --pattern '*.pkg.tar.zst' \
   129→  --pattern '*.AppImage' \
   130→  --pattern '*.snap' \
   131→  --pattern '*.tar.gz' \
   132→  --pattern '*.zip' \
   133→  --pattern '*.exe' \
   134→  --pattern '*.cdx.json' \
   135→  --pattern '*.spdx.json'
   136→
   137→ARTIFACTS=()
   138→while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
   139→  ARTIFACTS+=("$f")
   140→done < <(find "$WORK_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -type f \
   141→  \( -name '*.deb' -o -name '*.rpm' -o -name '*.pkg.tar.zst' \
   142→     -o -name '*.AppImage' -o -name '*.snap' -o -name '*.tar.gz' \
   143→     -o -name '*.zip' -o -name '*.exe' \
   144→     -o -name '*.cdx.json' -o -name '*.spdx.json' \) \
   145→  -print0 | sort -z)
   146→
   147→if [[ ${#ARTIFACTS[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
   148→  echo "error: no signable artifacts found in release $VERSION" >&2
   149→  exit 1
   150→fi
   151→
   152→echo ""
   153→echo "Artifacts to sign (${#ARTIFACTS[@]}):"
   154→for f in "${ARTIFACTS[@]}"; do
   155→  echo "  $(basename "$f")"
   156→done
   157→
   158→# --- Confirm ---
   159→if [[ "$SKIP_CONFIRM" != true ]]; then
   160→  echo ""
   161→  if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == true ]]; then
   162→    read -r -p "Sign these artifacts (dry-run, no upload)? [y/N] " confirm
   163→  else
   164→    read -r -p "Sign and upload .asc files to release $VERSION? [y/N] " confirm
   165→  fi
   166→  if [[ ! "$confirm" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
   167→    echo "Aborted."
   168→    exit 0
   169→  fi
   170→fi
   171→
   172→# --- Sign ---
   173→echo ""
   174→ASC_FILES=()
   175→for file in "${ARTIFACTS[@]}"; do
   176→  name="$(basename "$file")"
   177→  echo "Signing: $name"
   178→
   179→  # Verify existing checksum if available (defense against tampered downloads)
   180→  sha256_file="${WORK_DIR}/${name}.sha256"
   181→  if [[ -f "$sha256_file" ]]; then
   182→    echo "  Verifying SHA256..."
   183→  elif gh release download "$VERSION" --repo "$REPO" --dir "$WORK_DIR" --pattern "${name}.sha256" 2>/dev/null; then
   184→    sha256_file="${WORK_DIR}/${name}.sha256"
   185→  else
   186→    sha256_file=""
   187→  fi
   188→
   189→  if [[ -n "$sha256_file" && -f "$sha256_file" ]]; then
   190→    expected="$(awk '{print $1}' "$sha256_file")"
   191→    actual="$(sha256sum "$file" 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}' || shasum -a 256 "$file" | awk '{print $1}')"
   192→    if [[ "$expected" != "$actual" ]]; then
   193→      echo "  ERROR: SHA256 mismatch for $name!" >&2
   194→      echo "    expected: $expected" >&2
   195→      echo "    actual:   $actual" >&2
   196→      exit 1
   197→    fi
   198→    echo "  SHA256 verified"
   199→  else
   200→    echo "  WARNING: no SHA256 checksum found for $name — skipping integrity check" >&2
   201→  fi
   202→
   203→  gpg --batch "${GPG_SIGN_ARGS[@]}" --armor --detach-sign "$file"
   204→  ASC_FILES+=("${file}.asc")
   205→  echo "  Created: ${name}.asc"
   206→done
   207→
   208→echo ""
   209→echo "Signed ${#ASC_FILES[@]} artifacts."
   210→
   211→# --- Upload ---
   212→if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == true ]]; then
   213→  echo ""
   214→  echo "Dry run — skipping upload. Signatures in: $WORK_DIR"
   215→  echo "To upload manually:"
   216→  echo "  gh release upload $VERSION --repo $REPO ${WORK_DIR}/*.asc"
   217→  # Keep work dir on dry-run so user can inspect
   218→  trap - EXIT
   219→  exit 0
   220→fi
   221→
   222→echo ""
   223→echo "Uploading .asc files to release $VERSION..."
   224→
   225→# Check if any .asc files already exist on the release
   226→EXISTING_ASC="$(gh release view "$VERSION" --repo "$REPO" --json assets --jq '[.assets[].name | select(endswith(".asc"))] | join(", ")')"
   227→if [[ -n "$EXISTING_ASC" ]]; then
   228→  echo "  NOTE: replacing existing signatures: $EXISTING_ASC"
   229→fi
   230→
   231→gh release upload "$VERSION" \
   232→  --repo "$REPO" \
   233→  --clobber \
   234→  "${ASC_FILES[@]}"
   235→
   236→echo ""
   237→echo "Done! GPG signatures uploaded to release $VERSION."
   238→echo ""
   239→echo "Users can verify with:"
   240→echo "  ./scripts/verify-release.sh --version $VERSION"
   241→echo ""
   242→echo "Or manually:"
   243→echo "  curl -fsSL https://pen.so/gpg.asc | gpg --import"
   244→echo "  gpg --verify moltis-${VERSION}-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.asc \\"
   245→echo "               moltis-${VERSION}-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz"
   246→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→
     3→set -euo pipefail
     4→
     5→# Verify GPG signatures on Moltis release artifacts.
     6→#
     7→# Downloads the maintainer's public key from https://pen.so/gpg.asc,
     8→# then verifies the detached GPG signature (.asc) for each artifact
     9→# in the current directory or a specified release.
    10→#
    11→# Prerequisites:
    12→#   - gpg
    13→#   - curl (for key fetch) or gh (for release download)
    14→#
    15→# Usage:
    16→#   ./scripts/verify-release.sh [OPTIONS] [FILE...]
    17→#   ./scripts/verify-release.sh moltis-20260331.01-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz
    18→#   ./scripts/verify-release.sh --version 20260331.01
    19→#   ./scripts/verify-release.sh *.tar.gz *.deb
    20→
    21→GPG_KEY_URL="https://pen.so/gpg.asc"
    22→EXPECTED_FINGERPRINT="310320A8CC1C5BA86AD09040C0451BADF7649BBF"
    23→REPO="${MOLTIS_REPO:-moltis-org/moltis}"
    24→
    25→usage() {
    26→  cat <<'EOF'
    27→Usage: ./scripts/verify-release.sh [OPTIONS] [FILE...]
    28→
    29→Verifies GPG signatures on Moltis release artifacts.
    30→
    31→  FILE          One or more local artifact files to verify (must have
    32→                matching .asc files alongside them)
    33→
    34→Options:
    35→  -V, --version VER   Download and verify all artifacts for this release
    36→  -k, --key URL       GPG public key URL (default: https://pen.so/gpg.asc)
    37→  -s, --skip-key      Skip key import (already in keyring)
    38→  --checksums         Also verify SHA256 checksums
    39→  -h, --help          Show this help
    40→
    41→Environment:
    42→  MOLTIS_REPO         GitHub repo (default: moltis-org/moltis)
    43→
    44→Examples:
    45→  ./scripts/verify-release.sh --version 20260331.01
    46→  ./scripts/verify-release.sh --checksums --version 20260331.01
    47→  ./scripts/verify-release.sh moltis-*.tar.gz
    48→EOF
    49→}
    50→
    51→# --- Parse arguments ---
    52→VERSION=""
    53→SKIP_KEY=false
    54→VERIFY_CHECKSUMS=false
    55→FILES=()
    56→
    57→while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    58→  case "$1" in
    59→    -V|--version)  VERSION="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    60→    -k|--key)      GPG_KEY_URL="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    61→    -s|--skip-key) SKIP_KEY=true; shift ;;
    62→    --checksums)   VERIFY_CHECKSUMS=true; shift ;;
    63→    -h|--help)     usage; exit 0 ;;
    64→    -*)            echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; usage; exit 1 ;;
    65→    *)             FILES+=("$1"); shift ;;
    66→  esac
    67→done
    68→
    69→# --- Preflight ---
    70→if ! command -v gpg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    71→  echo "error: gpg is required but not found" >&2
    72→  exit 1
    73→fi
    74→
    75→# --- Import maintainer key ---
    76→if [[ "$SKIP_KEY" != true ]]; then
    77→  echo "Fetching maintainer GPG key from $GPG_KEY_URL..."
    78→  KEY_DATA="$(curl -fsSL "$GPG_KEY_URL")"
    79→  if [[ -z "$KEY_DATA" ]]; then
    80→    echo "error: failed to fetch GPG key from $GPG_KEY_URL" >&2
    81→    exit 1
    82→  fi
    83→  # Verify fingerprint before importing into the real keyring
    84→  ACTUAL_FINGERPRINT="$(echo "$KEY_DATA" | gpg --with-colons --import-options show-only --import 2>/dev/null \
    85→    | awk -F: '/^fpr/ { print $10; exit }')"
    86→  if [[ "$ACTUAL_FINGERPRINT" != "$EXPECTED_FINGERPRINT" ]]; then
    87→    echo "error: fetched key fingerprint does not match expected maintainer key" >&2
    88→    echo "  expected: $EXPECTED_FINGERPRINT" >&2
    89→    echo "  actual:   $ACTUAL_FINGERPRINT" >&2
    90→    exit 1
    91→  fi
    92→
    93→  echo "$KEY_DATA" | gpg --import 2>&1 || true
    94→  echo ""
    95→fi
    96→
    97→# --- Download release artifacts if --version given ---
    98→WORK_DIR=""
    99→if [[ -n "$VERSION" ]]; then
   100→  if ! command -v gh >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   101→    echo "error: gh (GitHub CLI) is required for --version download" >&2
   102→    exit 1
   103→  fi
   104→
   105→  if [[ ! "$VERSION" =~ ^[0-9]{8}\.[0-9]{1,2}$ ]]; then
   106→    echo "error: '$VERSION' does not match YYYYMMDD.NN format" >&2
   107→    exit 1
   108→  fi
   109→
   110→  WORK_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"
   111→  trap 'rm -rf "$WORK_DIR"' EXIT
   112→
   113→  echo "Downloading release artifacts for $VERSION..."
   114→  PATTERNS=(
   115→    '*.deb' '*.rpm' '*.pkg.tar.zst' '*.AppImage' '*.snap'
   116→    '*.tar.gz' '*.zip' '*.exe'
   117→    '*.cdx.json' '*.spdx.json'
   118→    '*.asc'
   119→  )
   120→  if [[ "$VERIFY_CHECKSUMS" == true ]]; then
   121→    PATTERNS+=('*.sha256')
   122→  fi
   123→
   124→  DL_ARGS=()
   125→  for p in "${PATTERNS[@]}"; do
   126→    DL_ARGS+=(--pattern "$p")
   127→  done
   128→
   129→  gh release download "$VERSION" \
   130→    --repo "$REPO" \
   131→    --dir "$WORK_DIR" \
   132→    "${DL_ARGS[@]}"
   133→
   134→  # Collect artifact files (not .asc/.sha256)
   135→  while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
   136→    FILES+=("$f")
   137→  done < <(find "$WORK_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -type f \
   138→    \( -name '*.deb' -o -name '*.rpm' -o -name '*.pkg.tar.zst' \
   139→       -o -name '*.AppImage' -o -name '*.snap' -o -name '*.tar.gz' \
   140→       -o -name '*.zip' -o -name '*.exe' \
   141→       -o -name '*.cdx.json' -o -name '*.spdx.json' \) \
   142→    -print0 | sort -z)
   143→fi
   144→
   145→if [[ ${#FILES[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
   146→  echo "error: no files to verify. Provide files or use --version." >&2
   147→  usage
   148→  exit 1
   149→fi
   150→
   151→# --- Verify ---
   152→PASS=0
   153→FAIL=0
   154→SKIP=0
   155→
   156→for file in "${FILES[@]}"; do
   157→  name="$(basename "$file")"
   158→  asc="${file}.asc"
   159→
   160→  echo "Verifying: $name"
   161→
   162→  # SHA256 checksum (optional)
   163→  if [[ "$VERIFY_CHECKSUMS" == true ]]; then
   164→    sha256_file="${file}.sha256"
   165→    if [[ -f "$sha256_file" ]]; then
   166→      expected="$(awk '{print $1}' "$sha256_file")"
   167→      actual="$(sha256sum "$file" 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}' || shasum -a 256 "$file" | awk '{print $1}')"
   168→      if [[ "$expected" != "$actual" ]]; then
   169→        echo "  FAIL: SHA256 mismatch" >&2
   170→        echo "    expected: $expected" >&2
   171→        echo "    actual:   $actual" >&2
   172→        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
   173→        continue
   174→      fi
   175→      echo "  SHA256: OK"
   176→    else
   177→      echo "  SHA256: no checksum file found" >&2
   178→    fi
   179→  fi
   180→
   181→  # GPG signature
   182→  if [[ ! -f "$asc" ]]; then
   183→    echo "  SKIP: no .asc signature file found" >&2
   184→    SKIP=$((SKIP + 1))
   185→    continue
   186→  fi
   187→
   188→  GPG_OUTPUT="$(gpg --batch --verify "$asc" "$file" 2>&1)" && GPG_RC=0 || GPG_RC=$?
   189→  if [[ $GPG_RC -eq 0 ]]; then
   190→    # Show the signer identity (e.g. "Good signature from ...")
   191→    echo "$GPG_OUTPUT" | grep -i 'good signature' | sed 's/^/  /' || echo "  GPG: OK"
   192→    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
   193→  else
   194→    echo "  FAIL: GPG signature verification failed" >&2
   195→    echo "$GPG_OUTPUT" | sed 's/^/    /' >&2
   196→    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
   197→  fi
   198→done
   199→
   200→# --- Summary ---
   201→echo ""
   202→echo "Results: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed, $SKIP skipped (${#FILES[@]} total)"
   203→
   204→if [[ $FAIL -gt 0 ]]; then
   205→  echo ""
   206→  echo "ERROR: $FAIL artifact(s) failed verification!" >&2
   207→  exit 1
   208→fi
   209→
   210→if [[ $PASS -eq 0 ]]; then
   211→  echo ""
   212→  echo "WARNING: no artifacts were verified (all skipped)" >&2
   213→  exit 1
   214→fi
   215→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→
     3→set -euo pipefail
     4→
     5→usage() {
     6→  cat <<'EOF'
     7→Usage: ./scripts/prepare-release.sh [version]
     8→
     9→Examples:
    10→  ./scripts/prepare-release.sh              # auto: YYYYMMDD.NN from today + next seq
    11→  ./scripts/prepare-release.sh 20260311.01  # explicit version
    12→
    13→Version format: YYYYMMDD.NN (date + two-digit daily sequence number).
    14→
    15→This command:
    16→1) generates release notes for <version> via git-cliff from unreleased commits,
    17→2) keeps a fresh empty [Unreleased] section at the top of CHANGELOG.md,
    18→3) syncs Cargo.lock via cargo fetch.
    19→EOF
    20→}
    21→
    22→if [[ $# -gt 1 ]]; then
    23→  usage
    24→  exit 1
    25→fi
    26→
    27→repo_root="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
    28→cd "$repo_root"
    29→
    30→if ! command -v git-cliff >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    31→  echo "git-cliff is required. Install it first (for example: cargo install git-cliff --locked)." >&2
    32→  exit 1
    33→fi
    34→
    35→if [[ ! -f Cargo.toml || ! -f CHANGELOG.md || ! -f cliff.toml ]]; then
    36→  echo "run this script from the repository root (Cargo.toml, CHANGELOG.md, cliff.toml required)" >&2
    37→  exit 1
    38→fi
    39→
    40→# Compute or validate version
    41→if [[ $# -eq 1 ]]; then
    42→  new_version="$1"
    43→  if ! [[ "$new_version" =~ ^[0-9]{8}\.[0-9]{1,2}$ ]]; then
    44→    echo "invalid version: '$new_version' (expected YYYYMMDD.NN)" >&2
    45→    exit 1
    46→  fi
    47→else
    48→  # Auto-compute: today's date + next sequence number
    49→  today="$(date -u +%Y%m%d)"
    50→  # Find highest existing seq for today's tags
    51→  max_seq=0
    52→  for tag in $(git tag -l "${today}.*" 2>/dev/null); do
    53→    seq="${tag#"${today}."}"
    54→    if [[ "$seq" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [[ "10#$seq" -gt "10#$max_seq" ]]; then
    55→      max_seq="$seq"
    56→    fi
    57→  done
    58→  next_seq=$((10#$max_seq + 1))
    59→  new_version="$(printf '%s.%02d' "$today" "$next_seq")"
    60→  echo "auto-computed version: $new_version"
    61→fi
    62→
    63→release_date="$(echo "$new_version" | sed 's/^\([0-9]\{4\}\)\([0-9]\{2\}\)\([0-9]\{2\}\)\..*/\1-\2-\3/')"
    64→
    65→if rg -q "^## \\[$new_version\\]" CHANGELOG.md; then
    66→  echo "CHANGELOG.md already contains version $new_version" >&2
    67→  exit 1
    68→fi
    69→
    70→release_section_tmp="$(mktemp)"
    71→if ! git-cliff \
    72→  --config cliff.toml \
    73→  --unreleased \
    74→  --tag "$new_version" \
    75→  --strip all \
    76→  > "$release_section_tmp"; then
    77→  rm -f "$release_section_tmp"
    78→  echo "failed to generate release notes via git-cliff" >&2
    79→  exit 1
    80→fi
    81→
    82→dated_release_section_tmp="$(mktemp)"
    83→if ! awk -v version="$new_version" -v date="$release_date" '
    84→BEGIN {
    85→  replaced = 0
    86→}
    87→{
    88→  if (replaced == 0 && $0 ~ ("^## \\[" version "\\] - [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$")) {
    89→    print "## [" version "] - " date
    90→    replaced = 1
    91→    next
    92→  }
    93→  print
    94→}
    95→END {
    96→  if (replaced == 0) {
    97→    exit 13
    98→  }
    99→}
   100→' "$release_section_tmp" > "$dated_release_section_tmp"; then
   101→  rc=$?
   102→  rm -f "$release_section_tmp" "$dated_release_section_tmp"
   103→  if [[ "$rc" -eq 13 ]]; then
   104→    echo "git-cliff output did not contain expected release header for version $new_version" >&2
   105→  fi
   106→  exit 1
   107→fi
   108→mv "$dated_release_section_tmp" "$release_section_tmp"
   109→
   110→changelog_tmp="$(mktemp)"
   111→if ! awk -v release_section_file="$release_section_tmp" '
   112→function print_empty_unreleased() {
   113→  print "## [Unreleased]"
   114→  print ""
   115→  print "### Added"
   116→  print ""
   117→  print "### Changed"
   118→  print ""
   119→  print "### Deprecated"
   120→  print ""
   121→  print "### Removed"
   122→  print ""
   123→  print "### Fixed"
   124→  print ""
   125→  print "### Security"
   126→}
   127→function print_release_section(   line) {
   128→  while ((getline line < release_section_file) > 0) {
   129→    print line
   130→  }
   131→  close(release_section_file)
   132→}
   133→BEGIN {
   134→  replaced = 0
   135→  skipping_old_unreleased = 0
   136→}
   137→{
   138→  if (replaced == 0 && $0 == "## [Unreleased]") {
   139→    print_empty_unreleased()
   140→    print ""
   141→    print_release_section()
   142→    print ""
   143→    replaced = 1
   144→    skipping_old_unreleased = 1
   145→    next
   146→  }
   147→  if (skipping_old_unreleased == 1) {
   148→    if ($0 ~ /^## \[[0-9]/) {
   149→      skipping_old_unreleased = 0
   150→      print
   151→    }
   152→    next
   153→  }
   154→  print
   155→}
   156→END {
   157→  if (replaced == 0) {
   158→    exit 12
   159→  }
   160→}
   161→' CHANGELOG.md > "$changelog_tmp"; then
   162→  rc=$?
   163→  rm -f "$release_section_tmp" "$changelog_tmp"
   164→  if [[ "$rc" -eq 12 ]]; then
   165→    echo "failed to locate '## [Unreleased]' in CHANGELOG.md" >&2
   166→  fi
   167→  exit 1
   168→fi
   169→mv "$changelog_tmp" CHANGELOG.md
   170→rm -f "$release_section_tmp"
   171→
   172→cargo fetch
   173→cargo fetch --locked
   174→
   175→# Rebuild changelog HTML for the website
   176→node website/scripts/build-changelog.mjs
   177→
   178→echo "Release prep complete:"
   179→echo "  version: $new_version"
   180→echo "  date:    $release_date"
   181→

1→name: CI
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    branches: [main]
     6→  pull_request:
     7→  workflow_dispatch:
     8→  schedule:
     9→    - cron: "0 4 * * *"
    10→
    11→concurrency:
    12→  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
    13→  cancel-in-progress: true
    14→
    15→permissions: {}
    16→
    17→env:
    18→  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
    19→  NIGHTLY_TOOLCHAIN: nightly-2025-11-30
    20→
    21→jobs:
    22→  local-validation:
    23→    name: ${{ matrix.label }}
    24→    if: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.actor != 'dependabot[bot]' }}
    25→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    26→    permissions:
    27→      contents: read
    28→      statuses: read
    29→    strategy:
    30→      fail-fast: false
    31→      matrix:
    32→        include:
    33→          - label: fmt
    34→            context: local/fmt
    35→          - label: biome
    36→            context: local/biome
    37→          - label: i18n
    38→            context: local/i18n
    39→          - label: zizmor
    40→            context: local/zizmor
    41→          - label: clippy
    42→            context: local/lint
    43→          - label: test
    44→            context: local/test
    45→          - label: macos-app
    46→            context: local/macos-app
    47→          - label: ios-app
    48→            context: local/ios-app
    49→    steps:
    50→      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
    51→        with:
    52→          persist-credentials: false
    53→      - name: Verify local status context
    54→        env:
    55→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
    56→          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
    57→          PR_HEAD_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
    58→          REQUIRED_CONTEXT: ${{ matrix.context }}
    59→        run: python3 scripts/check-local-status.py
    60→
    61→  changelog-guard:
    62→    name: Changelog Guard
    63→    if: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}
    64→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    65→    permissions:
    66→      contents: read
    67→    steps:
    68→      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
    69→        with:
    70→          persist-credentials: false
    71→          fetch-depth: 0
    72→      - name: Block manual CHANGELOG.md edits
    73→        env:
    74→          BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
    75→          HEAD_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
    76→        run: bash ./scripts/check-changelog-guard.sh "$BASE_SHA" "$HEAD_SHA"
    77→
    78→  zizmor:
    79→    name: Workflow Security
    80→    if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' }}
    81→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    82→    permissions:
    83→      contents: read
    84→    steps:
    85→      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
    86→        with:
    87→          persist-credentials: false
    88→      - uses: zizmorcore/zizmor-action@135698455da5c3b3e55f73f4419e481ab68cdd95 # v0.4.1
    89→        with:
    90→          advanced-security: false
    91→          online-audits: false
    92→
    93→  biome:
    94→    name: Biome
    95→    if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' }}
    96→    needs: zizmor
    97→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    98→    permissions:
    99→      contents: read
   100→    steps:
   101→      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
   102→        with:
   103→          persist-credentials: false
   104→      - uses: biomejs/setup-biome@29711cbb52afee00eb13aeb30636592f9edc0088 # v2.7.0
   105→        with:
   106→          version: "2.4.6"
   107→      - run: biome ci crates/web/src/assets/js/
   108→      - run: ./scripts/i18n-check.sh
   109→
   110→  fmt:
   111→    name: Format
   112→    if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' }}
   113→    needs: zizmor
   114→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   115→    permissions:
   116→      contents: read
   117→    steps:
   118→      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
   119→        with:
   120→          persist-credentials: false
   121→      - name: Check website install script sync
   122→        run: ./scripts/check-website-install-sync.sh
   123→      - name: Check install script package names match release workflow
   124→        run: ./scripts/check-install-package-names.sh
   125→      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@f7ccc83f9ed1e5b9c81d8a67d7ad1a747e22a561 # master
   126→        with:
   127→          toolchain: ${{ env.NIGHTLY_TOOLCHAIN }}
   128→          components: rustfmt
   129→      - run: cargo fmt --all -- --check
   130→
   131→  rust-ci:
   132→    name: Rust CI (clippy + test)
   133→    if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' }}
   134→    needs: [fmt, biome]
   135→    runs-on: [self-hosted, Linux, X64]
   136→    permissions:
   137→      contents: read
   138→    container:
   139→      image: nvidia/cuda:12.4.1-devel-ubuntu22.04
   140→    env:
   141→      LD_LIBRARY_PATH: /usr/local/cuda/compat:/usr/local/nvidia/lib:/usr/local/nvidia/lib64
   142→    steps:
   143→      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
   144→        with:
   145→          persist-credentials: false
   146→      - name: Clean up corrupted cargo config
   147→        run: rm -f ~/.cargo/config.toml
   148→      - name: Install build dependencies
   149→        run: |
   150→          apt-get update
   151→          apt-get install -y curl git cmake build-essential clang libclang-dev pkg-config ca-certificates wget gpg
   152→          wget -qO- https://packages.lunarg.com/lunarg-signing-key-pub.asc | tee /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/lunarg.asc >/dev/null
   153→          echo "deb https://packages.lunarg.com/vulkan jammy main" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/lunarg-vulkan-jammy.list
   154→          apt-get update
   155→          apt-get install -y vulkan-sdk
   156→          nvcc --version
   157→      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@f7ccc83f9ed1e5b9c81d8a67d7ad1a747e22a561 # master
   158→        with:
   159→          toolchain: ${{ env.NIGHTLY_TOOLCHAIN }}
   160→          components: clippy
   161→      - name: Initialize git repo in llama-cpp source to satisfy cmake
   162→        run: |
   163→          cargo fetch --locked
   164→          LLAMA_SRC=$(find ~/.cargo/registry/src -name "llama-cpp-sys-2-*" -type d 2>/dev/null | head -1)
   165→          if [ -n "$LLAMA_SRC" ] && [ ! -d "$LLAMA_SRC/.git" ]; then
   166→            cd "$LLAMA_SRC"
   167→            git init
   168→            git config user.email "ci@example.com"
   169→            git config user.name "CI"
   170→            git add -A
   171→            git commit -m "init" --allow-empty
   172→            git tag v0.0.0
   173→          fi
   174→      - name: Build Tailwind CSS
   175→        run: |
   176→          ./scripts/download-tailwindcss-cli.sh tailwindcss-linux-x64
   177→          cd crates/web/ui && TAILWINDCSS=../../../tailwindcss-linux-x64 ./build.sh
   178→      - run: cargo clippy -Z unstable-options --workspace --all-features --all-targets --timings -- -D warnings
   179→      - name: Upload cargo timing reports
   180→        if: always()
   181→        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
   182→        with:
   183→          name: cargo-timings-rust-ci-${{ github.run_id }}-${{ github.run_attempt }}
   184→          path: target/cargo-timings/
   185→          if-no-files-found: ignore
   186→      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@f176c07a0a40cbfdd08ee9aa8bf1655701d11e69 # v2.67.25
   187→        with:
   188→          tool: cargo-nextest
   189→      - run: cargo nextest run --all-features --profile ci
   190→      - name: Upload test results
   191→        if: always()
   192→        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
   193→        with:
   194→          name: test-results-${{ github.run_id }}-${{ github.run_attempt }}
   195→          path: target/nextest/ci/junit.xml
   196→          if-no-files-found: ignore
   197→
   198→  coverage:
   199→    name: Code Coverage
   200→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

   480→      - name: Build Tailwind CSS

   492→      - name: Determine package version

   531→

   544→        with:
   545→          name: moltis-${{ matrix.arch }}.pkg.tar.zst
   546→          path: |
   547→            *.pkg.tar.zst

   549→            *.pkg.tar.zst.sha512

   555→    strategy:

   563→            arch: aarch64

   575→        with:

   578→      - name: Install Rust toolchain

   583→
   584→      - name: Install cosign
   585→        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
   586→        uses: sigstore/cosign-installer@d7d6bc7722e3daa8354c50bcb52f4837da5e9b6a # v3.8.1
   587→
   588→      - name: Build Tailwind CSS
   589→        run: |
   590→          ARCH=$(uname -m)
   591→          case "$ARCH" in x86_64) TW="tailwindcss-linux-x64";; aarch64) TW="tailwindcss-linux-arm64";; esac
   592→          ./scripts/download-tailwindcss-cli.sh "$TW"
   593→          cd crates/web/ui && TAILWINDCSS="../../../$TW" ./build.sh
   594→
   595→      - name: Build WASM components
   596→        run: |
   597→          cargo build --target wasm32-wasip2 -p moltis-wasm-calc -p moltis-wasm-web-fetch -p moltis-wasm-web-search --release
   598→          cargo run -p moltis-wasm-precompile --release
   599→

600→      - name: Determine package version
   601→        id: version
   602→        run: |
   603→          if [[ "$GITHUB_REF" == refs/tags/* ]]; then
   604→            VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
   605→          else
   606→            VERSION="0.0.0-dev"
   607→          fi
   608→          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
   609→
   610→      - name: Build release binary
   611→        env:
   612→          BUILD_TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
   613→          MOLTIS_VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
   614→        run: cargo build --release --target "$BUILD_TARGET"
   615→
   616→      - name: Build AppImage
   617→        env:
   618→          VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
   619→          BUILD_TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
   620→          MATRIX_ARCH: ${{ matrix.arch }}
   621→          APPIMAGETOOL_ARCH: ${{ matrix.appimagetool_arch }}
   622→        run: |
   623→          # Download appimagetool matching the runner architecture
   624→          wget -q "https://github.com/AppImage/appimagetool/releases/download/continuous/appimagetool-${APPIMAGETOOL_ARCH}.AppImage" -O appimagetool
   625→          chmod +x appimagetool
   626→
   627→          # Build AppDir structure
   628→          APP_DIR="moltis.AppDir"
   629→          mkdir -p "$APP_DIR/usr/bin" "$APP_DIR/usr/share/moltis/web" "$APP_DIR/usr/share/moltis/wasm"
   630→          cp "target/$BUILD_TARGET/release/moltis" "$APP_DIR/usr/bin/moltis"
   631→          chmod 755 "$APP_DIR/usr/bin/moltis"
   632→          cp -R crates/web/src/assets/. "$APP_DIR/usr/share/moltis/web/"
   633→          cp "target/wasm32-wasip2/release/moltis_wasm_calc.wasm" "$APP_DIR/usr/share/moltis/wasm/"
   634→          cp "target/wasm32-wasip2/release/moltis_wasm_web_fetch.wasm" "$APP_DIR/usr/share/moltis/wasm/"
   635→          cp "target/wasm32-wasip2/release/moltis_wasm_web_search.wasm" "$APP_DIR/usr/share/moltis/wasm/"
   636→
   637→          # Create .desktop file
   638→          cat > "$APP_DIR/moltis.desktop" <<DESKTOP
   639→          [Desktop Entry]
   640→          Type=Application
   641→          Name=Moltis
   642→          Exec=moltis
   643→          Icon=moltis
   644→          Categories=Network;
   645→          Terminal=true
   646→          DESKTOP
   647→
   648→          # Create minimal icon
   649→          cat > "$APP_DIR/moltis.svg" <<SVG
   650→          <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="256" height="256"><rect width="256" height="256" fill="#333"/><text x="128" y="140" font-size="120" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">M</text></svg>
   651→          SVG
   652→          ln -s moltis.svg "$APP_DIR/.DirIcon"
   653→
   654→          # Create AppRun
   655→          cat > "$APP_DIR/AppRun" <<'APPRUN'
   656→          #!/bin/sh
   657→          SELF=$(readlink -f "$0")
   658→          HERE=${SELF%/*}
   659→          export MOLTIS_SHARE_DIR="$HERE/usr/share/moltis"
   660→          exec "$HERE/usr/bin/moltis" "$@"
   661→          APPRUN
   662→          chmod +x "$APP_DIR/AppRun"
   663→
   664→          # Package - use --appimage-extract-and-run to avoid FUSE requirement in CI
   665→          ARCH="$MATRIX_ARCH" ./appimagetool --appimage-extract-and-run "$APP_DIR" "moltis-${VERSION}-${MATRIX_ARCH}.AppImage"
   666→
   667→      - name: Sign with Sigstore and generate checksums

   687→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   688→    name: Build Snap
   689→    permissions:
   690→      contents: read
   691→      id-token: write # Required for Sigstore keyless signing
   692→
   693→    steps:
   694→      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
   695→        with:
   696→          persist-credentials: false
   697→
   698→      - name: Resolve release version
   699→        id: release_version
   700→        run: |
   701→          if [[ "$GITHUB_REF" == refs/tags/* ]]; then
   702→            VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
   703→          else
   704→            VERSION="0.0.0-dev"
   705→          fi
   706→          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
   707→
   708→      - name: Sync snap version to release version
   709→        env:
   710→          VERSION: ${{ steps.release_version.outputs.version }}

   714→      - name: Install cosign

   739→  build-homebrew-binaries:

   744→          - target: x86_64-apple-darwin
   745→            os: macos-latest
   746→          - target: aarch64-apple-darwin
   747→            os: macos-latest
   748→          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu

   750→          - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
   751→            os: ubuntu-24.04-arm

   753→    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
   754→    name: Build binary (${{ matrix.target }})
   755→    permissions:
   756→      contents: read
   757→      id-token: write # Required for Sigstore keyless signing

   761→        with:
   762→          persist-credentials: false

   764→      - name: Install Rust toolchain
   765→        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@f7ccc83f9ed1e5b9c81d8a67d7ad1a747e22a561 # master

   773→
   774→      - name: Build Tailwind CSS
   775→        run: |
   776→          OS=$(uname -s)
   777→          ARCH=$(uname -m)
   778→          case "${OS}-${ARCH}" in
   779→            Linux-x86_64)  TW="tailwindcss-linux-x64";;
   780→            Linux-aarch64) TW="tailwindcss-linux-arm64";;
   781→            Darwin-arm64)  TW="tailwindcss-macos-arm64";;
   782→            Darwin-x86_64) TW="tailwindcss-macos-x64";;
   783→          esac
   784→          ./scripts/download-tailwindcss-cli.sh "$TW"
   785→          cd crates/web/ui && TAILWINDCSS="../../../$TW" ./build.sh
   786→
   787→      - name: Build WASM components
   788→        run: |
   789→          cargo build --target wasm32-wasip2 -p moltis-wasm-calc -p moltis-wasm-web-fetch -p moltis-wasm-web-search --release
   790→          cargo run -p moltis-wasm-precompile --release

   792→      - name: Determine package version
   793→        id: version
   794→        run: |
   795→          if [[ "$GITHUB_REF" == refs/tags/* ]]; then
   796→            VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
   797→          else

   803→        env:
