   813→          PKG_DIR="moltis-package"

   815→          cp "target/$BUILD_TARGET/release/moltis" "$PKG_DIR/moltis"

   817→          cp "target/wasm32-wasip2/release/moltis_wasm_calc.wasm" "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/wasm/"
   818→          cp "target/wasm32-wasip2/release/moltis_wasm_web_fetch.wasm" "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/wasm/"

   820→          tar czf "moltis-${VERSION}-${BUILD_TARGET}.tar.gz" -C "$PKG_DIR" moltis share
   821→
   822→      - name: Sign with Sigstore and generate checksums
   823→        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
   824→        uses: ./.github/actions/sign-artifacts
   825→        with:
   826→          files: moltis-${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-${{ matrix.target }}.tar.gz

   828→      - name: Upload binary artifact

   834→            *.tar.gz
   835→            *.tar.gz.sha256
   836→            *.tar.gz.sha512

  1008→        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
  1009→        with:

  1017→
  1018→  build-docker:
  1019→    if: ${{ !(github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.dry_run) }}
  1020→    needs: [clippy, test, e2e]
  1021→    strategy:
  1022→      matrix:
  1023→        include:
  1024→          - platform: linux/amd64
  1025→            os: ubuntu-latest
  1026→          - platform: linux/arm64
  1027→            os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
  1028→
  1029→    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
  1030→    name: Build Docker (${{ matrix.platform }})
  1031→    permissions:
  1032→      contents: read
  1033→      packages: write
  1034→      id-token: write # Required for Sigstore keyless signing
  1035→      attestations: write # Required for provenance attestations
  1036→
  1037→    outputs:
  1038→      # Each matrix leg overwrites, but merge job reads digests from artifacts
  1039→      image: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}
  1040→
  1041→    steps:
  1042→      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
  1043→        with:
  1044→          persist-credentials: false
  1045→
  1046→      - name: Resolve release version
  1047→        id: release_version
  1048→        run: |
  1049→          if [[ "$GITHUB_REF" == refs/tags/* ]]; then
  1050→            VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
  1051→          else
  1052→            VERSION="0.0.0-dev"
  1053→          fi
  1054→          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
  1055→
  1056→      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
  1057→        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@b5ca514318bd6ebac0fb2aedd5d36ec1b5c232a2 # v3
  1058→
  1059→      - name: Log in to GitHub Container Registry
  1060→        uses: docker/login-action@74a5d142397b4f367a81961eba4e8cd7edddf772 # v3
  1061→        with:
  1062→          registry: ghcr.io
  1063→          username: ${{ github.actor }}
  1064→          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
  1065→
  1066→      - name: Extract metadata
  1067→        id: meta
  1068→        uses: docker/metadata-action@902fa8ec7d6ecbf8d84d538b9b233a880e428804 # v5
  1069→        with:
  1070→          images: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}
  1071→
  1072→      - name: Build and push by digest
  1073→        id: build
  1074→        uses: docker/build-push-action@14487ce63c7a62a4a324b0bfb37086795e31c6c1 # v6
  1075→        env:
  1076→          DOCKER_BUILD_RECORD_UPLOAD: "false"
  1077→        with:
  1078→          context: .
  1079→          platforms: ${{ matrix.platform }}
  1080→          build-args: MOLTIS_VERSION=${{ steps.release_version.outputs.version }}
  1081→          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
  1082→          outputs: type=image,"name=ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}",push-by-digest=true,name-canonical=true,push=true
  1083→          cache-from: type=gha,scope=${{ matrix.platform }}
  1084→          cache-to: type=gha,scope=${{ matrix.platform }},mode=max
  1085→          sbom: true
  1086→          provenance: mode=max
  1087→
  1088→      - name: Smoke test Docker image startup
  1089→        env:
  1090→          DIGEST: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
  1091→        run: |
  1092→          set -euo pipefail
  1093→          IMAGE="ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}@${DIGEST}"
  1094→
  1095→          docker run --rm "${IMAGE}" --help > /dev/null
  1096→
  1097→          DOCKER_CLI_VERSION="$(docker run --rm --entrypoint docker "${IMAGE}" --version | awk '{print $3}' | tr -d ',')"
  1098→          DOCKER_CLI_MAJOR="${DOCKER_CLI_VERSION%%.*}"
  1099→
  1100→          if [[ -z "${DOCKER_CLI_MAJOR}" || "${DOCKER_CLI_MAJOR}" -lt 25 ]]; then
  1101→            echo "Docker CLI in image is too old: ${DOCKER_CLI_VERSION} (require >= 25.x; API >= 1.44)" >&2
  1102→            exit 1
  1103→          fi
  1104→
  1105→      - name: Export digest
  1106→        env:
  1107→          DIGEST: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
  1108→        run: |
  1109→          mkdir -p /tmp/digests
  1110→          digest="${DIGEST#sha256:}"
  1111→          touch "/tmp/digests/${digest}"
  1112→
  1113→      - name: Upload digest
  1114→        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
  1115→        with:
  1116→          name: docker-digests-${{ strategy.job-index }}
  1117→          path: /tmp/digests/*
  1118→          if-no-files-found: error
  1119→          retention-days: 1
  1120→
  1121→  merge-docker:
  1122→    if: ${{ !(github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.dry_run) }}
  1123→    needs: build-docker
  1124→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  1125→    name: Merge Docker manifest
  1126→    permissions:
  1127→      contents: read
  1128→      packages: write
  1129→      id-token: write # Required for Sigstore keyless signing
  1130→
  1131→    steps:
  1132→      - name: Download digests
  1133→        uses: actions/download-artifact@d3f86a106a0bac45b974a628896c90dbdf5c8093 # v4
  1134→        with:
  1135→          path: /tmp/digests
  1136→          pattern: docker-digests-*
  1137→          merge-multiple: true
  1138→
  1139→      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
  1140→        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@b5ca514318bd6ebac0fb2aedd5d36ec1b5c232a2 # v3
  1141→
  1142→      - name: Install cosign
  1143→        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
  1144→        uses: sigstore/cosign-installer@d7d6bc7722e3daa8354c50bcb52f4837da5e9b6a # v3.8.1
  1145→
  1146→      - name: Log in to GitHub Container Registry
  1147→        uses: docker/login-action@74a5d142397b4f367a81961eba4e8cd7edddf772 # v3
  1148→        with:
  1149→          registry: ghcr.io
  1150→          username: ${{ github.actor }}
  1151→          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
  1152→
  1153→      - name: Check if this is the highest release tag
  1154→        id: check_latest
  1155→        env:
  1156→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
  1157→        run: |
  1158→          CURRENT="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
  1159→          LATEST=$(gh api "repos/${{ github.repository }}/tags" --paginate --jq '.[].name' \
  1160→            | grep -E '^[0-9]{8}\.[0-9]{2}$' \
  1161→            | sort | tail -1)
  1162→          if [ "$CURRENT" = "$LATEST" ]; then
  1163→            echo "is_latest=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
  1164→          else
  1165→            echo "is_latest=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
  1166→            echo "::notice::Skipping floating tags: ${CURRENT} is not the highest version (${LATEST} is)"
  1167→          fi
  1168→
  1169→      - name: Extract metadata
  1170→        id: meta
  1171→        uses: docker/metadata-action@902fa8ec7d6ecbf8d84d538b9b233a880e428804 # v5
  1172→        with:

1→name: Sign with Sigstore
     2→description: Generate checksums and sign files with Sigstore keyless signing
     3→
     4→inputs:
     5→  files:
     6→    description: Glob pattern or space-separated list of files to sign
     7→    required: true
     8→  working-directory:
     9→    description: Directory containing files (defaults to current directory)
    10→    required: false
    11→    default: '.'
    12→  skip-sha512:
    13→    description: Skip SHA512 checksum generation (only generate SHA256)
    14→    required: false
    15→    default: 'false'
    16→
    17→runs:
    18→  using: composite
    19→  steps:
    20→    - shell: bash
    21→      working-directory: ${{ inputs.working-directory }}
    22→      env:
    23→        FILES_PATTERN: ${{ inputs.files }}
    24→        SKIP_SHA512: ${{ inputs.skip-sha512 }}
    25→      run: |
    26→        # Function to generate checksum (handles macOS vs Linux)
    27→        checksum256() {
    28→          sha256sum "$1" > "${1}.sha256" 2>/dev/null || shasum -a 256 "$1" > "${1}.sha256"
    29→        }
    30→        checksum512() {
    31→          sha512sum "$1" > "${1}.sha512" 2>/dev/null || shasum -a 512 "$1" > "${1}.sha512"
    32→        }
    33→
    34→        # Process each file matching the pattern
    35→        for file in $FILES_PATTERN; do
    36→          if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
    37→            echo "Warning: No files matching '$file'"
    38→            continue
    39→          fi
    40→          echo "Signing: $file"
    41→
    42→          # Generate checksums
    43→          checksum256 "$file"
    44→          if [ "$SKIP_SHA512" != "true" ]; then
    45→            checksum512 "$file"
    46→          fi
    47→
    48→          # Sign with Sigstore (keyless)
    49→          cosign sign-blob --yes \
    50→            --output-signature "${file}.sig" \
    51→            --output-certificate "${file}.crt" \
    52→            "$file"
    53→        done

1→# Security Policy
     2→
     3→## Reporting a Vulnerability
     4→
     5→If you discover a security vulnerability in Moltis, please report it responsibly.
     6→
     7→### Preferred Methods
     8→
     9→1. **GitHub Security Advisories** (recommended): Use [GitHub's private vulnerability reporting](https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/security/advisories/new) to report issues confidentially.
    10→
    11→2. **Email**: Send details to [moltis AT pen DOT so](mailto:moltis AT pen DOT so)
    12→
    13→### What to Include
    14→
    15→- Description of the vulnerability
    16→- Steps to reproduce
    17→- Potential impact
    18→- Any suggested fixes (optional)
    19→
    20→### Scope
    21→
    22→This policy applies to the Moltis codebase. Third-party dependencies should be reported to their respective maintainers.
    23→
    24→## Supported Versions
    25→
    26→Security updates are provided for the latest release only.
    27→
    28→## Verifying Release Signatures
    29→
    30→All release artifacts are signed using [Sigstore](https://sigstore.dev) keyless
    31→signing. This provides cryptographic proof that artifacts were built by our
    32→GitHub Actions workflow, not tampered with after the fact.
    33→
    34→### Install cosign
    35→
    36→```bash
    37→# macOS
    38→brew install cosign
    39→
    40→# Linux (or download from https://github.com/sigstore/cosign/releases)
    41→go install github.com/sigstore/cosign/v2/cmd/cosign@latest
    42→```
    43→
    44→### Verify a binary/package
    45→
    46→Each release artifact has three companion files:
    47→- `.sha256` / `.sha512` — checksums
    48→- `.sig` — Sigstore signature
    49→- `.crt` — Signing certificate
    50→
    51→```bash
    52→# Download the artifact and its signature files

   121→

   149→```
   150→
   151→### Verifying your setup
   152→

   157→# Verify it's signed

   160→# Clean up

   163→
   164→### Troubleshooting
   165→
   166→If commits show as "Unverified" on GitHub:
   167→1. Ensure your signing key is added to your GitHub account
   168→2. Your commit email must match a verified email on your GitHub account
   169→3. For GPG: the key's email must match your commit email

Excellent! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document of all findings:

## Summary: Complete Release Pipeline and Security Documentation

Based on my thorough exploration of the Moltis codebase, here's the complete release pipeline and security setup:

---

### **1. GitHub Actions Workflow Files**

**Location:** `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/mixed-oil/.github/workflows/`

All workflows found:
- **ci.yml** — Main CI pipeline (format, biome, clippy, tests, coverage)
- **release.yml** — Complete release build and publish workflow (19K lines)
- **docs.yml** — Documentation deployment
- **e2e.yml** — End-to-end tests
- **codspeed.yml** — Performance benchmarking
- **homebrew.yml** — Homebrew integration

---

### **2. Release Pipeline Architecture (`.github/workflows/release.yml`)**

The release workflow is triggered by:
- **Tagged releases** (`refs/tags/YYYYMMDD.NN` format)
- **Manual dispatch** with optional `dry_run` or `pre_release` flags

#### **Release Stages:**

1. **Validation** (runs for all)
   - Zizmor: Workflow security audit
   - Biome: JS/TS linting
   - Rustfmt: Code formatting
   - Clippy: Rust linting
   - Unit tests
   - E2E tests

2. **Build Packages** (multi-platform)
   - `.deb` (x86_64, aarch64) via `cargo-deb`
   - `.rpm` (x86_64, aarch64) via `cargo-generate-rpm`
   - `.pkg.tar.zst` (Arch Linux, x86_64, aarch64)
   - `.AppImage` (x86_64, aarch64)
   - `.snap` (via snapcraft)
   - Binary `.tar.gz` (4 targets: Linux x86_64/aarch64, macOS x86_64/aarch64)
   - **macOS app** (`.app.zip`)
   - **Windows exe** (x86_64-pc-windows-msvc)
   - **Docker images** (linux/amd64, linux/arm64) with multi-arch manifest

3. **Signing** (all artifacts)
   - Uses `.github/actions/sign-artifacts` custom action
   - Generates SHA256 & SHA512 checksums
   - **Sigstore keyless signing** via cosign (creates `.sig` + `.crt` files)
   - **No private keys** — uses GitHub OIDC tokens

4. **Upload & Release**
   - Collects all artifacts (binaries, checksums, signatures)
   - Uploads to GitHub Releases via `softprops/action-gh-release`
   - CHANGELOG.md included
   - Supports pre-release flag

5. **Additional Steps** (only for non-dry-run, non-pre-release)
   - **Generate SBOM**: CycloneDX JSON + SPDX JSON format
   - **Sign SBOM**: Sigstore signatures on both
   - **Update Homebrew tap**: `moltis-org/homebrew-tap` repository (requires `HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN`)
   - **Update deploy templates**: `.do/deploy.template.yaml`, `render.yaml`, `fly.toml`, `website/releases.json` on main branch
   - **Docker attestations**: SLSA provenance `mode=max` + SBOM attached to images

---

### **3. Signing Architecture**

#### **Sigstore Keyless Signing** (`.github/actions/sign-artifacts/action.yml`)

Each artifact gets:
- `.sha256` — SHA256 checksum
- `.sha512` — SHA512 checksum
- `.sig` — Detached Sigstore signature
- `.crt` — Sigstore signing certificate (OIDC-bound)

```bash
cosign sign-blob --yes \
  --output-signature "${file}.sig" \
  --output-certificate "${file}.crt" \
  "$file"
```

**What it proves:**
- Artifact was built by GitHub Actions in `moltis-org/moltis` repo
- Build triggered by a specific commit
- No tampering post-build
- Timestamps recorded in Sigstore's Rekor transparency log

#### **Docker Image Signing**

- **SBOM**: Attached via `cosign download sbom ghcr.io/moltis-org/moltis:latest`
- **Provenance**: SLSA provenance attestation via `provenance: mode=max` in buildx
- **Container signature**: Signed with `cosign sign`
- **Verification**: `cosign verify-attestation --type slsaprovenance`

#### **Optional GPG Signing** (YubiKey-resident)

The workflow supports **additional** GPG signing for maintainer authorization via:
- `scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh` — Signs all artifacts post-CI
- `scripts/verify-release.sh` — User verification tool
- Maintainer fingerprint: `3103 20A8 CC1C 5BA8 6AD0 9040 C045 1BAD F764 9BBF`
- Public key hosted at: `https://pen.so/gpg.asc`

---

### **4. Release Scripts**

#### **`scripts/prepare-release.sh`**

Prepares a release locally:
- Auto-computes version `YYYYMMDD.NN` (or accepts explicit version)
- Generates release notes via `git-cliff` (conventional commits → structured changelog)
- Updates `CHANGELOG.md` with new release section
- Keeps empty `[Unreleased]` section at top
- Runs `cargo fetch --locked` to sync lockfile
- Rebuilds `website/scripts/build-changelog.mjs`

Usage:
```bash
./scripts/prepare-release.sh              # auto-compute
./scripts/prepare-release.sh 20260405.05  # explicit
```

#### **`scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh`**

Signs already-published release artifacts with YubiKey:
- Downloads artifacts from GitHub release
- Verifies SHA256 checksums (defense against tampering)
- Creates `.asc` detached GPG signatures
- Uploads `.asc` files back to release (can clobber old signatures)
- Supports `--dry-run` (sign locally, don't upload)

Prerequisites:
- `gh` (GitHub CLI) authenticated
- GPG with YubiKey inserted
- `GPG_KEY_ID` env var or `--key` flag

Usage:
```bash
./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh                  # latest release
./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh 20260331.01      # specific version
./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh --dry-run 20260331.01
```

#### **`scripts/verify-release.sh`**

Verifies artifact signatures (Sigstore + GPG):
- Downloads maintainer's public key automatically (fingerprint validated)
- Verifies Sigstore signatures (`.sig` + `.crt`)
- Verifies GPG signatures (`.asc`)
- Optional SHA256 checksum verification
- Can verify from release or local files

Usage:
```bash
./scripts/verify-release.sh --version 20260331.01
./scripts/verify-release.sh --checksums --version 20260331.01
./scripts/verify-release.sh moltis-*.tar.gz  # local files
```

---

### **5. Documentation**

#### **`docs/src/security.md`** (comprehensive 590-line document)

Core topics:
- **Command Execution Approval** — Human-in-the-loop, approval modes (always/smart/never)
- **Dangerous Command Blocklist** — Hardcoded regex patterns + allowlist override
- **Destructive Command Guard (dcg)** — Optional hook for extended pattern coverage
- **Sandbox Isolation** — Docker/Apple Container, resource limits, network isolation
- **Channel Authorization** — Sender allowlisting, per-channel permissions
- **Cron Job Security** — Rate limiting (10 jobs/min default), audit events
- **Identity Protection** — YAML frontmatter in IDENTITY.md/USER.md/SOUL.md (requires `operator.write`)
- **API Authorization** — Role-based scopes (`operator.read`, `operator.write`, `operator.admin`, `operator.approvals`, `operator.pairing`)
- **API Keys** — Least-privilege by default, must specify scopes
- **Encryption at Rest** — XChaCha20-Poly1305 + Argon2id vault
- **TLS & Origin Validation** — Auto-generated certs, CSWSH prevention
- **SSRF Protection** — DNS resolution, blocks private/loopback/CGNAT (allowlist available)
- **HTTP Throttling** — Per-IP rate limits on auth endpoints
- **Reverse Proxy Deployments** — `MOLTIS_BEHIND_PROXY=true`, WebAuthn RP ID configuration
- **Production Recommendations** — Auth, sandbox, rate limits, monitoring, network segmentation
- **Release Signing & Verification** (dual-layer):
  - Sigstore keyless (CI-generated)
  - GPG signing (maintainer-authorized, YubiKey-resident)

#### **`docs/src/release-verification.md`** (167-line document)

**Verification methods:**

1. **Quick verification** (recommended):
   ```bash
   ./scripts/verify-release.sh --version VERSION
   ./scripts/verify-release.sh --checksums --version VERSION
   ```

2. **Manual verification steps:**
   - Download artifact + checksum + signature files
   - Verify SHA256 with `sha256sum --check`
   - Import maintainer's GPG key (fingerprinted)
   - Verify GPG signature: `gpg --verify artifact.asc artifact`
   - Verify Cosign signature: `cosign verify-blob`
   - Verify Docker images: `cosign verify`

3. **Artifact file extensions per layer:**
   - `.sha256` / `.sha512` — CI (checksums)
   - `.sig` / `.crt` — CI (Sigstore)
   - `.asc` — Maintainer (GPG)

4. **Maintainer signing workflow** (post-CI):
   - Release must be published by CI first
   - Maintainer runs `gpg-sign-release.sh` with YubiKey
   - YubiKey PIN/tap required for each signature

---

### **6. Version Format & Changelog**

- **Version format:** `YYYYMMDD.NN` (date-based, two-digit daily sequence)
- **Cargo.toml:** Stays at static `0.1.0`; real version injected via `MOLTIS_VERSION` env var
- **Changelog:** Generated from conventional commits via `git-cliff`
- **Conventional commit types:** `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`, `ci`, `style` → auto-categorized
- **Security commits:** Special group, auto-moved to end
- **No manual CHANGELOG edits:** PR CI enforces this via `scripts/check-changelog-guard.sh`

---

### **7. SBOM & Attestations**

**Generated during release:**
- **CycloneDX JSON** (`moltis-sbom.cdx.json`) — Signed + checksummed
- **SPDX JSON** (`moltis-sbom.spdx.json`) — Signed + checksummed
- **Docker SBOM** — Attached to container images (queryable via `cosign download sbom`)
- **Docker Provenance** — SLSA provenance attestation (`provenance: mode=max` in Docker buildx)

---

### **8. Security Policy** (`SECURITY.md`)

- **Vulnerability reporting:** GitHub Security Advisories (private) or email
- **Supported versions:** Latest release only
- **Commit signing:** Required (SSH or GPG)
- **Sigstore transparency log:** All signatures searchable at `https://search.sigstore.dev`
- **No private keys in CI:** OIDC tokens bound to GitHub Actions identity

---

### **9. Key Files Summary**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `.github/workflows/release.yml` | Complete multi-platform release pipeline |
| `.github/actions/sign-artifacts/action.yml` | Sigstore signing + checksum generation |
| `scripts/prepare-release.sh` | Local release prep (changelog, version, lockfile) |
| `scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh` | Maintainer post-CI GPG signing via YubiKey |
| `scripts/verify-release.sh` | User verification script (Sigstore + GPG) |
| `docs/src/security.md` | Complete security architecture (590 lines) |
| `docs/src/release-verification.md` | Detailed verification instructions |
| `SECURITY.md` | Policy + quick verification guide |
| `cliff.toml` | Conventional commit → changelog mapping |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Auto-generated release notes |

---

### **10. Release Flow (Two-Phase)**

**Phase 1 — Local Prep + Push:**
```bash
./scripts/prepare-release.sh              # Generates changelog, bumps version
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: prepare release YYYYMMDD.NN"
git tag YYYYMMDD.NN
git push --follow-tags                    # CI builds, signs with Sigstore, uploads release
```

**Phase 2 — Post-CI GPG Signing (maintainer, after CI completes):**
```bash
./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh [VERSION]   # YubiKey prompts for PIN/tap
                                          # Uploads .asc files to release
```

**Verification (users):**
```bash
./scripts/verify-release.sh --version YYYYMMDD.NN  # Downloads + verifies all signatures
```

---

This is a **production-grade, supply-chain hardened release pipeline** with:
- ✓ Dual signing (Sigstore CI + GPG maintainer)
- ✓ Multi-platform build & sign
- ✓ SBOM & provenance attestations
- ✓ Transparent audit trail (Rekor)
- ✓ No private keys in CI (OIDC-bound)
- ✓ Conventional commits + auto-changelog
- ✓ Checksum integrity verification
- ✓ User-friendly verification scripts

Usage:
```bash
./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh                  # latest release
./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh 20260331.01      # specific version
./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh --dry-run 20260331.01
```

#### **`scripts/verify-release.sh`**

Verifies artifact signatures (Sigstore + GPG):
- Downloads maintainer's public key automatically (fingerprint validated)
- Verifies Sigstore signatures (`.sig` + `.crt`)
- Verifies GPG signatures (`.asc`)
- Optional SHA256 checksum verification
- Can verify from release or local files

Usage:
```bash
./scripts/verify-release.sh --version 20260331.01
./scripts/verify-release.sh --checksums --version 20260331.01
./scripts/verify-release.sh moltis-*.tar.gz  # local files
```

---

### **5. Documentation**

#### **`docs/src/security.md`** (comprehensive 590-line document)

Core topics:
- **Command Execution Approval** — Human-in-the-loop, approval modes (always/smart/never)
- **Dangerous Command Blocklist** — Hardcoded regex patterns + allowlist override
- **Destructive Command Guard (dcg)** — Optional hook for extended pattern coverage
- **Sandbox Isolation** — Docker/Apple Container, resource limits, network isolation
- **Channel Authorization** — Sender allowlisting, per-channel permissions
- **Cron Job Security** — Rate limiting (10 jobs/min default), audit events
- **Identity Protection** — YAML frontmatter in IDENTITY.md/USER.md/SOUL.md (requires `operator.write`)
- **API Authorization** — Role-based scopes (`operator.read`, `operator.write`, `operator.admin`, `operator.approvals`, `operator.pairing`)
- **API Keys** — Least-privilege by default, must specify scopes
- **Encryption at Rest** — XChaCha20-Poly1305 + Argon2id vault
- **TLS & Origin Validation** — Auto-generated certs, CSWSH prevention
- **SSRF Protection** — DNS resolution, blocks private/loopback/CGNAT (allowlist available)
- **HTTP Throttling** — Per-IP rate limits on auth endpoints
- **Reverse Proxy Deployments** — `MOLTIS_BEHIND_PROXY=true`, WebAuthn RP ID configuration
- **Production Recommendations** — Auth, sandbox, rate limits, monitoring, network segmentation
- **Release Signing & Verification** (dual-layer):
  - Sigstore keyless (CI-generated)
  - GPG signing (maintainer-authorized, YubiKey-resident)

#### **`docs/src/release-verification.md`** (167-line document)

**Verification methods:**

1. **Quick verification** (recommended):
   ```bash
   ./scripts/verify-release.sh --version VERSION
   ./scripts/verify-release.sh --checksums --version VERSION
   ```

2. **Manual verification steps:**
   - Download artifact + checksum + signature files
   - Verify SHA256 with `sha256sum --check`
   - Import maintainer's GPG key (fingerprinted)
   - Verify GPG signature: `gpg --verify artifact.asc artifact`
   - Verify Cosign signature: `cosign verify-blob`
   - Verify Docker images: `cosign verify`

3. **Artifact file extensions per layer:**
   - `.sha256` / `.sha512` — CI (checksums)
   - `.sig` / `.crt` — CI (Sigstore)
   - `.asc` — Maintainer (GPG)

4. **Maintainer signing workflow** (post-CI):
   - Release must be published by CI first
   - Maintainer runs `gpg-sign-release.sh` with YubiKey
   - YubiKey PIN/tap required for each signature

---

### **6. Version Format & Changelog**

- **Version format:** `YYYYMMDD.NN` (date-based, two-digit daily sequence)
- **Cargo.toml:** Stays at static `0.1.0`; real version injected via `MOLTIS_VERSION` env var
- **Changelog:** Generated from conventional commits via `git-cliff`
- **Conventional commit types:** `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`, `ci`, `style` → auto-categorized
- **Security commits:** Special group, auto-moved to end
- **No manual CHANGELOG edits:** PR CI enforces this via `scripts/check-changelog-guard.sh`

---

### **7. SBOM & Attestations**

**Generated during release:**
- **CycloneDX JSON** (`moltis-sbom.cdx.json`) — Signed + checksummed
- **SPDX JSON** (`moltis-sbom.spdx.json`) — Signed + checksummed
- **Docker SBOM** — Attached to container images (queryable via `cosign download sbom`)
- **Docker Provenance** — SLSA provenance attestation (`provenance: mode=max` in Docker buildx)

---

### **8. Security Policy** (`SECURITY.md`)

- **Vulnerability reporting:** GitHub Security Advisories (private) or email
- **Supported versions:** Latest release only
- **Commit signing:** Required (SSH or GPG)
- **Sigstore transparency log:** All signatures searchable at `https://search.sigstore.dev`
- **No private keys in CI:** OIDC tokens bound to GitHub Actions identity

---

### **9. Key Files Summary**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `.github/workflows/release.yml` | Complete multi-platform release pipeline |
| `.github/actions/sign-artifacts/action.yml` | Sigstore signing + checksum generation |
| `scripts/prepare-release.sh` | Local release prep (changelog, version, lockfile) |
| `scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh` | Maintainer post-CI GPG signing via YubiKey |
| `scripts/verify-release.sh` | User verification script (Sigstore + GPG) |
| `docs/src/security.md` | Complete security architecture (590 lines) |
| `docs/src/release-verification.md` | Detailed verification instructions |
| `SECURITY.md` | Policy + quick verification guide |
| `cliff.toml` | Conventional commit → changelog mapping |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Auto-generated release notes |

---

### **10. Release Flow (Two-Phase)**

**Phase 1 — Local Prep + Push:**
```bash
./scripts/prepare-release.sh              # Generates changelog, bumps version
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: prepare release YYYYMMDD.NN"
git tag YYYYMMDD.NN
git push --follow-tags                    # CI builds, signs with Sigstore, uploads release
```

**Phase 2 — Post-CI GPG Signing (maintainer, after CI completes):**
```bash
./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh [VERSION]   # YubiKey prompts for PIN/tap
                                          # Uploads .asc files to release
```

**Verification (users):**
```bash
./scripts/verify-release.sh --version YYYYMMDD.NN  # Downloads + verifies all signatures
```

---

This is a **production-grade, supply-chain hardened release pipeline** with:
- ✓ Dual signing (Sigstore CI + GPG maintainer)
- ✓ Multi-platform build & sign
- ✓ SBOM & provenance attestations
- ✓ Transparent audit trail (Rekor)
- ✓ No private keys in CI (OIDC-bound)
- ✓ Conventional commits + auto-changelog
- ✓ Checksum integrity verification
- ✓ User-friendly verification scripts

content.content:
# Secretive Release Workflow with Artifact Attestations

## Workflow Structure

The release workflow triggers on git tag pushes and consists of two jobs: `test` and `build`.

## Key Components

**Permissions (build job):**
- `id-token: write` - Required for OIDC token generation
- `contents: write` - For creating releases
- `attestations: write` - For generating build provenance
- `actions: read` - For artifact access

**Attestation Generation:**

The workflow uses `actions/attest-build-provenance@v2` to create build provenance:

```
- name: Attest
  id: attest
  uses: actions/attest-build-provenance@v2
  with:
    subject-path: "Secretive.zip"
```

This step generates a cryptographic attestation proving the artifact's provenance.

**Attestation Integration:**

The attestation ID is captured and embedded in the release:

```
ATTESTATION_ID: ${{ steps.attest.outputs.attestation-id }}
```

This value is then substituted into the release notes template via `sed` commands before publishing.

## Artifact Handling

1. Build creates an archive: `Archive.xcarchive`
2. App is copied to artifact folder
3. `actions/upload-artifact@v4` stages the build output
4. Artifact is downloaded via GitHub API using a bearer token
5. Downloaded zip is notarized with Apple's tools
6. Attestation is generated for the final zip
7. Both artifact and attestation ID are published in the GitHub release

This approach ensures released binaries have verifiable cryptographic proof of their build origin and construction process.

content.content:
# Secretive: Artifact Attestations in README

## Auditable Build Process Section

The README includes a dedicated section on artifact attestations:

> "Starting with Secretive 3.0, builds are attested using GitHub Artifact Attestation"

The documentation explains that attestations serve as proof of the build's integrity and can be viewed in two locations:

1. **Build logs** - Individual attestations are visible in each build's GitHub Actions workflow log
2. **Attestation page** - A centralized [attestations page](https://github.com/maxgoedjen/secretive/attestations) displays all project attestations

## Verification Context

The project emphasizes transparency by highlighting its use of GitHub's artifact attestation feature as part of its "auditable build and release generation process." This approach allows users to independently verify that released binaries correspond to publicly visible source code and build steps, rather than trusting claims about the build process alone.

The README positions attestations as a security feature demonstrating commitment to supply chain integrity for version 3.0 and later releases.

content.content:
# GitHub Artifact Attestations Documentation Extract

## What Artifact Attestations Are

According to the documentation, artifact attestations enable you to establish "*unfalsifiable provenance and integrity guarantees for the software you build*." They create cryptographically signed claims that include:

- Links to associated workflows
- Repository, organization, environment, commit SHA, and triggering event details
- OIDC token information for provenance establishment

## SLSA Framework Level

The documentation states that "*Artifact attestations by itself provides SLSA v1.0 Build Level 2*." Achieving Build Level 3 requires using reusable workflows alongside attestations.

## Implementation Details Not Covered

**Important limitation:** The provided documentation does **not** include:
- Specific GitHub Actions step syntax (actions/attest-build-provenance, etc.)
- Required permissions configuration
- Generation examples for binaries, containers, or SBOMs
- The `gh attestation verify` command syntax
- YAML workflow examples
- Configuration parameters

The content references a separate guide titled "Using artifact attestations to establish provenance for builds" as the implementation resource, but that guide isn't included in your source material.

## When to Generate Attestations

You should sign releases and binaries users will run, but **not** sign frequent test builds or individual source files.

## Verification Guidance

The documentation emphasizes that "*artifact attestations are not a guarantee that an artifact is secure*" but rather link you to source code and build instructions for policy enforcement.

file.content:
name: Build Packages

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "[0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9].[0-9][0-9]"
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      dry_run:
        description: "Build release packages without publishing artifacts, images, or release assets"
        required: false
        default: false
        type: boolean
      pre_release:
        description: "Publish as pre-release (builds and uploads artifacts but skips Homebrew tap, deploy templates, and releases.json updates)"
        required: false
        default: false
        type: boolean

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions: {}

env:
  NIGHTLY_TOOLCHAIN: nightly-2025-11-30
  RELEASE_DRY_RUN: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.dry_run && 'true' || 'false' }}
  RELEASE_PRE_RELEASE: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.pre_release && 'true' || 'false' }}

jobs:
  zizmor:
    name: Workflow Security
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Dry-run short-circuit
        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN == 'true' }}
        run: echo "Dry run enabled, skipping workflow security checks"

      - uses: zizmorcore/zizmor-action@135698455da5c3b3e55f73f4419e481ab68cdd95 # v0.4.1
        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
        with:
          advanced-security: false
          online-audits: false

  validate-tag:
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Validate Tag Format
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Check tag matches YYYYMMDD.NN
        env:
          TAG: ${{ github.ref_name }}
        run: |
          if [[ ! "$TAG" =~ ^[0-9]{8}\.[0-9]{2}$ ]]; then
            echo "::error::Tag '$TAG' must match YYYYMMDD.NN format"
            exit 1
          fi

  biome:
    needs: zizmor
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Biome
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
      - name: Dry-run short-circuit
        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN == 'true' }}
        run: echo "Dry run enabled, skipping biome checks"
      - uses: biomejs/setup-biome@29711cbb52afee00eb13aeb30636592f9edc0088 # v2.7.0
        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
        with:
          version: "2.4.6"
      - if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
        run: biome ci crates/web/src/assets/js/
      - if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
        run: ./scripts/i18n-check.sh

  fmt:
    needs: zizmor
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Format
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
      - name: Dry-run short-circuit

file.content:
enabled = true
backend = "auto"  # uses strongest available
```

### 3. Limit Rate Limits

Tighten rate limits for untrusted environments:

```toml
[cron]
rate_limit_max = 5
rate_limit_window_secs = 300  # 5 per 5 minutes
```

### 4. Review Channel Senders

Regularly audit approved senders and revoke access for unknown parties.

### 5. Monitor Events

Watch for these suspicious patterns:

- Rapid cron job creation
- Identity modification attempts
- Unusual command patterns in approval requests
- New channel senders from unexpected sources

### 6. Network Segmentation

Run Moltis on a private network or behind a reverse proxy with:

- IP allowlisting
- Rate limiting
- Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules

### 7. Keep Software Updated

Subscribe to security advisories and update promptly when vulnerabilities are
disclosed.

## Release Signing and Verification

All release artifacts are signed with two independent methods:

1. **Sigstore keyless signing** (automated in CI) — proves the artifact was
   built by the `moltis-org/moltis` GitHub Actions pipeline
2. **GPG signing** (maintainer's YubiKey hardware key) — proves a specific
   maintainer authorized the release

Checksums (SHA-256 and SHA-512) are generated for every artifact.

See [Release Verification](release-verification.md) for detailed verification
instructions, artifact file extensions, and maintainer signing workflow.

## Reporting Security Issues

Report security vulnerabilities privately to the maintainers. Do not open
public issues for security bugs.

See the repository's SECURITY.md for contact information.

file.content:
# Release Verification

Moltis releases use **dual signing** to provide strong supply chain guarantees:

| Method | Proves | Verification |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| **Sigstore** (keyless, CI-generated) | Artifact was built by GitHub Actions from this repo | `cosign verify-blob` |
| **GPG** (YubiKey-resident key, maintainer-signed) | A specific maintainer authorized the release | `gpg --verify` |
| **SHA256/SHA512 checksums** | File integrity (no corruption/tampering in transit) | `sha256sum --check` |

## Quick Verification

The easiest way to verify a release is with the included script:

```bash
# Verify all artifacts for a release (fetches GPG key automatically)
./scripts/verify-release.sh --version VERSION

# Also check SHA256 checksums
./scripts/verify-release.sh --checksums --version VERSION

# Verify specific local files
./scripts/verify-release.sh moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz
```

### Manual Verification

#### 1. Verify checksums

```bash
# Download the artifact and its checksum
curl -LO https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/releases/download/VERSION/moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz
curl -LO https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/releases/download/VERSION/moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.sha256

sha256sum --check moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.sha256
```

#### 2. Verify GPG signature

The maintainer's GPG key fingerprint is:

```
3103 20A8 CC1C 5BA8 6AD0  9040 C045 1BAD F764 9BBF
```

```bash
# Import the maintainer's public key (one-time)
curl -fsSL https://pen.so/gpg.asc | gpg --import

# Confirm the fingerprint matches
gpg --fingerprint F7649BBF

# Download the detached signature
curl -LO https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/releases/download/VERSION/moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.asc

# Verify
gpg --verify \
  moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.asc \
  moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz
```

You should see `Good signature from ...` with the maintainer's identity.

#### 3. Verify Sigstore signature

```bash
# Install cosign: https://docs.sigstore.dev/cosign/system_config/installation/

# Download signature and certificate
curl -LO https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/releases/download/VERSION/moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.sig
curl -LO https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/releases/download/VERSION/moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.crt

cosign verify-blob \
  --signature moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.sig \
  --certificate moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.crt \
  --certificate-oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com \
  --certificate-identity-regexp 'https://github\.com/moltis-org/moltis/' \
  moltis-VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz
```

#### 4. Verify Docker images

```bash
cosign verify \
  --certificate-oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com \
  --certificate-identity-regexp 'https://github\.com/moltis-org/moltis/' \
  ghcr.io/moltis-org/moltis:VERSION
```

## What Each Layer Proves

**Checksums** detect download corruption or CDN tampering. They do not prove
who created the file.

**Sigstore signatures** prove the artifact was built inside the
`moltis-org/moltis` GitHub Actions workflow using OIDC-based keyless signing.
This guards against a compromised maintainer laptop — even if someone steals
credentials, they cannot reproduce a valid Sigstore certificate from the CI
environment.

**GPG signatures** prove the release was reviewed and authorized by a specific
maintainer holding the corresponding private key. Because the key lives on a
YubiKey hardware token, compromise requires physical access to the device plus
the PIN.

Together, the two signatures create a strong chain: Sigstore proves *where* the
artifact was built (CI), and GPG proves *who* authorized it (maintainer with
hardware key).

## Release Artifacts Per File

Each release artifact (`.deb`, `.rpm`, `.tar.gz`, `.exe`, etc.) may have:

| Extension | Source | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `.sha256` | CI | SHA-256 checksum |
| `.sha512` | CI | SHA-512 checksum |
| `.sig` | CI | Sigstore detached signature |
| `.crt` | CI | Sigstore signing certificate |
| `.asc` | Maintainer | GPG detached armored signature |

## For Maintainers: Signing a Release

After CI publishes a release, sign the artifacts locally using your
YubiKey-resident GPG key:

```bash
# Sign the latest release (prompts for YubiKey PIN/touch)
./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh

# Sign a specific version
./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh 20260331.01

# Use a specific key
./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh --key 0xABCD1234 20260331.01

# Dry run (sign locally, don't upload)
./scripts/gpg-sign-release.sh --dry-run 20260331.01
```

The script:
1. Downloads all release packages from GitHub
2. Verifies their SHA256 checksums against CI-generated values
3. Creates `.asc` detached GPG signatures for each artifact
4. Uploads the `.asc` files to the GitHub release

### Prerequisites

- [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/) (`gh`) authenticated
- GPG with your signing key available (YubiKey inserted)
- The `GPG_KEY_ID` environment variable or `--key` flag if you have
  multiple secret keys

### Publish Your Public Key

Upload your public GPG key so users can verify signatures:

```bash
# Export and upload to a keyserver
gpg --export --armor YOUR_KEY_ID | \
  curl -T - https://keys.openpgp.org

# Also add to your GitHub profile:
# Settings > SSH and GPG keys > New GPG key
gpg --export --armor YOUR_KEY_ID
```

# Artifact Attestations Implementation Guide

## Action Version & Parameters

The documentation references **`actions/attest@v4`** (not v2). Key parameters include:

- `subject-path`: Path to binary artifact
- `subject-name`: Fully-qualified container image name (without tag)
- `subject-digest`: SHA256 digest in format `sha256:HEX_DIGEST`
- `sbom-path`: Path to JSON-formatted SBOM file
- `push-to-registry`: Boolean to push attestations to registry

## Required Permissions

**For binaries:**
```yaml
permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read
  attestations: write
```

**For container images:**
```yaml
permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read
  attestations: write
  packages: write
```

**For linked artifacts page integration:**
Add `artifact-metadata: write` permission plus `push-to-registry: true`

## Attestation Types

### Build Provenance - Binaries
```yaml
- name: Generate artifact attestation
  uses: actions/attest@v4
  with:
    subject-path: 'PATH/TO/ARTIFACT'
```

### Build Provenance - Container Images
```yaml
- name: Generate artifact attestation
  uses: actions/attest@v4
  with:
    subject-name: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
    subject-digest: 'sha256:fedcba0...'
    push-to-registry: true
```

Key requirement: "Do not include a tag as part of the image name."

### SBOM Attestations
Add `sbom-path` parameter to either workflow above.

## Verification with GitHub CLI

**Binaries:**
```bash
gh attestation verify PATH/TO/YOUR/BUILD/ARTIFACT-BINARY -R ORG/REPO
```

**Container images:**
```bash
docker login ghcr.io
gh attestation verify oci://ghcr.io/ORG/IMAGE:test -R ORG/REPO
```

**SBOM (SPDX format):**
```bash
gh attestation verify PATH/TO/ARTIFACT -R ORG/REPO \
  --predicate-type https://spdx.dev/Document/v2.3
```

## Critical Requirements

- Create SBOM first (using tools like `anchore-sbom-action`)
- For container images, obtain digest from `docker/build-push-action` output
- Attestations visible in repository **Actions** tab
- Repository must support GitHub Actions

## No Gotchas Listed

The documentation does not specify repository configuration requirements beyond standard GitHub Actions setup.

1237→  upload-release:
  1238→    needs:
  1239→      - build-deb
  1240→      - build-rpm
  1241→      - build-arch
  1242→      - build-appimage
  1243→      - build-snap
  1244→      - build-homebrew-binaries
  1245→      - build-macos-app
  1246→      - build-windows-exe
  1247→    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !(github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.dry_run)
  1248→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  1249→    name: Upload release assets
  1250→    permissions:
  1251→      contents: write
  1252→
  1253→    steps:
  1254→      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
  1255→        with:
  1256→          persist-credentials: false
  1257→          sparse-checkout: CHANGELOG.md
  1258→          sparse-checkout-cone-mode: false
  1259→
  1260→      - name: Download all build artifacts
  1261→        uses: actions/download-artifact@d3f86a106a0bac45b974a628896c90dbdf5c8093 # v4
  1262→        with:
  1263→          path: artifacts
  1264→          pattern: moltis-*
  1265→
  1266→      - name: Download snap artifact
  1267→        uses: actions/download-artifact@d3f86a106a0bac45b974a628896c90dbdf5c8093 # v4
  1268→        with:
  1269→          name: moltis.snap
  1270→          path: artifacts/moltis.snap
  1271→
  1272→      - name: Collect all release files
  1273→        run: |
  1274→          mkdir -p release-files
  1275→          find artifacts -type f \( \
  1276→            -name '*.deb' -o -name '*.rpm' -o -name '*.pkg.tar.zst' \
  1277→            -o -name '*.AppImage' -o -name '*.snap' -o -name '*.tar.gz' -o -name '*.zip' -o -name '*.exe' \
  1278→            -o -name '*.sha256' -o -name '*.sha512' -o -name '*.sig' -o -name '*.crt' \
  1279→          \) -exec cp {} release-files/ \;
  1280→          cp CHANGELOG.md release-files/
  1281→          echo "Files to upload:"
  1282→          ls -lh release-files/
  1283→
  1284→      - name: Upload to release

file.content:
upload-release:
    needs:
      - build-deb
      - build-rpm
      - build-arch
      - build-appimage
      - build-snap
      - build-homebrew-binaries
      - build-macos-app
      - build-windows-exe
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !(github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.dry_run)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Upload release assets
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
          sparse-checkout: CHANGELOG.md
          sparse-checkout-cone-mode: false

      - name: Download all build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@d3f86a106a0bac45b974a628896c90dbdf5c8093 # v4
        with:
          path: artifacts
          pattern: moltis-*

      - name: Download snap artifact
        uses: actions/download-artifact@d3f86a106a0bac45b974a628896c90dbdf5c8093 # v4
        with:
          name: moltis.snap
          path: artifacts/moltis.snap

      - name: Collect all release files
        run: |
          mkdir -p release-files
          find artifacts -type f \( \
            -name '*.deb' -o -name '*.rpm' -o -name '*.pkg.tar.zst' \
            -o -name '*.AppImage' -o -name '*.snap' -o -name '*.tar.gz' -o -name '*.zip' -o -name '*.exe' \
            -o -name '*.sha256' -o -name '*.sha512' -o -name '*.sig' -o -name '*.crt' \
          \) -exec cp {} release-files/ \;
          cp CHANGELOG.md release-files/
          echo "Files to upload:"
          ls -lh release-files/

      - name: Upload to release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@a06a81a03ee405af7f2048a818ed3f03bbf83c7b # v2
        with:
          files: release-files/*
          prerelease: ${{ env.RELEASE_PRE_RELEASE == 'true' }}

  # Generate SBOM for the entire release
  generate-sbom:
    needs:
      - upload-release
      - merge-docker
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !(github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.dry_run)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Generate Release SBOM
    permissions:
      contents: write
      id-token: write # Required for Sigstore keyless signing

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Install cosign
        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
        uses: sigstore/cosign-installer@d7d6bc7722e3daa8354c50bcb52f4837da5e9b6a # v3.8.1

      - name: Install cargo-sbom
        run: cargo install cargo-sbom

      - name: Generate SBOM (CycloneDX and SPDX)
        run: |
          cargo sbom --output-format cyclone_dx_json_1_4 > moltis-sbom.cdx.json
          cargo sbom --output-format spdx_json_2_3 > moltis-sbom.spdx.json

      - name: Sign SBOMs with Sigstore and generate checksums
        uses: ./.github/actions/sign-artifacts
        with:
          files: moltis-sbom.cdx.json moltis-sbom.spdx.json
          skip-sha512: 'true'

      - name: Upload SBOM to release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@a06a81a03ee405af7f2048a818ed3f03bbf83c7b # v2
        with:
          files: |
            moltis-sbom.cdx.json
            moltis-sbom.cdx.json.sha256
            moltis-sbom.cdx.json.sig
            moltis-sbom.cdx.json.crt
            moltis-sbom.spdx.json
            moltis-sbom.spdx.json.sha256
            moltis-sbom.spdx.json.sig
            moltis-sbom.spdx.json.crt

  update-homebrew-tap:
    needs:
      - upload-release
      - merge-docker
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !(github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && (inputs.dry_run || inputs.pre_release))
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Update Homebrew tap
    permissions:
      contents: read

file.content:
build-docker:
    if: ${{ !(github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.dry_run) }}
    needs: [clippy, test, e2e]
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - platform: linux/amd64
            os: ubuntu-latest
          - platform: linux/arm64
            os: ubuntu-24.04-arm

    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    name: Build Docker (${{ matrix.platform }})
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
      id-token: write # Required for Sigstore keyless signing
      attestations: write # Required for provenance attestations

    outputs:
      # Each matrix leg overwrites, but merge job reads digests from artifacts
      image: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Resolve release version
        id: release_version
        run: |
          if [[ "$GITHUB_REF" == refs/tags/* ]]; then
            VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
          else
            VERSION="0.0.0-dev"
          fi
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@b5ca514318bd6ebac0fb2aedd5d36ec1b5c232a2 # v3

      - name: Log in to GitHub Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@74a5d142397b4f367a81961eba4e8cd7edddf772 # v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@902fa8ec7d6ecbf8d84d538b9b233a880e428804 # v5
        with:
          images: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}

      - name: Build and push by digest
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@14487ce63c7a62a4a324b0bfb37086795e31c6c1 # v6
        env:
          DOCKER_BUILD_RECORD_UPLOAD: "false"
        with:
          context: .
          platforms: ${{ matrix.platform }}
          build-args: MOLTIS_VERSION=${{ steps.release_version.outputs.version }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          outputs: type=image,"name=ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}",push-by-digest=true,name-canonical=true,push=true
          cache-from: type=gha,scope=${{ matrix.platform }}
          cache-to: type=gha,scope=${{ matrix.platform }},mode=max
          sbom: true
          provenance: mode=max

      - name: Smoke test Docker image startup
        env:
          DIGEST: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          IMAGE="ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}@${DIGEST}"

          docker run --rm "${IMAGE}" --help > /dev/null

          DOCKER_CLI_VERSION="$(docker run --rm --entrypoint docker "${IMAGE}" --version | awk '{print $3}' | tr -d ',')"
          DOCKER_CLI_MAJOR="${DOCKER_CLI_VERSION%%.*}"

          if [[ -z "${DOCKER_CLI_MAJOR}" || "${DOCKER_CLI_MAJOR}" -lt 25 ]]; then
            echo "Docker CLI in image is too old: ${DOCKER_CLI_VERSION} (require >= 25.x; API >= 1.44)" >&2
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Export digest
        env:
          DIGEST: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
        run: |
          mkdir -p /tmp/digests
          digest="${DIGEST#sha256:}"
          touch "/tmp/digests/${digest}"

      - name: Upload digest
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
        with:
          name: docker-digests-${{ strategy.job-index }}
          path: /tmp/digests/*
          if-no-files-found: error
          retention-days: 1

  merge-docker:
    if: ${{ !(github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.dry_run) }}
    needs: build-docker
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Merge Docker manifest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
      id-token: write # Required for Sigstore keyless signing

    steps:
      - name: Download digests
        uses: actions/download-artifact@d3f86a106a0bac45b974a628896c90dbdf5c8093 # v4
        with:
          path: /tmp/digests
          pattern: docker-digests-*
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@b5ca514318bd6ebac0fb2aedd5d36ec1b5c232a2 # v3

      - name: Install cosign
        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
        uses: sigstore/cosign-installer@d7d6bc7722e3daa8354c50bcb52f4837da5e9b6a # v3.8.1

      - name: Log in to GitHub Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@74a5d142397b4f367a81961eba4e8cd7edddf772 # v3

      - name: Generate SBOM (CycloneDX and SPDX)

          cargo sbom --output-format spdx_json_2_3 > moltis-sbom.spdx.json

      - name: Sign SBOMs with Sigstore and generate checksums

      - name: Upload SBOM to release

            moltis-sbom.spdx.json
            moltis-sbom.spdx.json.sha256
            moltis-sbom.spdx.json.sig
            moltis-sbom.spdx.json.crt

  update-homebrew-tap:
    needs:

100→| Auth & Security | `moltis-auth`, `moltis-oauth`, `moltis-onboarding`, `moltis-vault` | 6.6K |
   101→| Networking | `moltis-network-filter`, `moltis-tls`, `moltis-tailscale` | 3.5K |

| Auth & Security | `moltis-auth`, `moltis-oauth`, `moltis-onboarding`, `moltis-vault` | 6.6K |
| Networking | `moltis-network-filter`, `moltis-tls`, `moltis-tailscale` | 3.5K |
| Provider setup | `moltis-provider-setup` | 4.3K |
| Import | `moltis-openclaw-import` | 7.6K |
| Apple native | `moltis-swift-bridge` | 2.1K |
| Metrics | `moltis-metrics` | 1.7K |
| Other | `moltis-projects`, `moltis-media`, `moltis-routing`, `moltis-canvas`, `moltis-auto-reply`, `moltis-schema-export`, `moltis-benchmarks` | 2.5K |

Use `--no-default-features --features lightweight` for constrained devices (Raspberry Pi, etc.).

## Security

- **Zero `unsafe` code\*** — denied workspace-wide; only opt-in FFI behind `local-embeddings` flag
- **Sandboxed execution** — Docker + Apple Container, per-session isolation
- **Secret handling** — `secrecy::Secret`, zeroed on drop, redacted from tool output
- **Authentication** — password + passkey (WebAuthn), rate-limited, per-IP throttle
- **SSRF protection** — DNS-resolved, blocks loopback/private/link-local
- **Origin validation** — rejects cross-origin WebSocket upgrades
- **Hook gating** — `BeforeToolCall` hooks can inspect/block any tool invocation

See [Security Architecture](https://docs.moltis.org/security.html) for details.

## Features

- **AI Gateway** — Multi-provider LLM support (OpenAI Codex, GitHub Copilot, Local), streaming responses, agent loop with sub-agent delegation, parallel tool execution
- **Communication** — Web UI, Telegram, Microsoft Teams, Discord, API access, voice I/O (8 TTS + 7 STT providers), mobile PWA with push notifications
- **Memory & Recall** — Per-agent memory workspaces, embeddings-powered long-term memory, hybrid vector + full-text search, session persistence with auto-compaction, cross-session recall, Cursor-compatible project context, context-file safety scanning
- **Safer Agent Editing** — Automatic checkpoints before built-in skill and memory mutations, restore tooling, session branching
- **Extensibility** — MCP servers (stdio + HTTP/SSE), skill system, 15 lifecycle hook events with circuit breaker, destructive command guard
- **Security** — Encryption-at-rest vault (XChaCha20-Poly1305 + Argon2id), password + passkey + API key auth, sandbox isolation, SSRF/CSWSH protection
- **Operations** — Cron scheduling, OpenTelemetry tracing, Prometheus metrics, cloud deploy (Fly.io, DigitalOcean), Tailscale integration, managed SSH deploy keys, host-pinned remote targets, live tool inventory in Settings, and CLI/web remote-exec doctor flows

## How It Works

Moltis is a **local-first persistent agent server** — a single Rust binary that
sits between you and multiple LLM providers, keeps durable session state, and
can meet you across channels without handing your data to a cloud relay.

```
┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐
│   Web UI    │  │  Telegram   │  │  Discord    │
└──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘
       │                │                │
       └────────┬───────┴────────┬───────┘
                │   WebSocket    │
                ▼                ▼
        ┌─────────────────────────────────┐
        │          Gateway Server         │
        │   (Axum · HTTP · WS · Auth)     │
        ├─────────────────────────────────┤
        │        Chat Service             │
        │  ┌───────────┐ ┌─────────────┐  │
        │  │   Agent   │ │    Tool     │  │
        │  │   Runner  │◄┤   Registry  │  │
        │  └─────┬─────┘ └─────────────┘  │
        │        │                        │
        │  ┌─────▼─────────────────────┐  │
        │  │    Provider Registry      │  │
        │  │  Multiple providers       │  │
        │  │  (Codex · Copilot · Local)│  │

file.content:
uses: docker/login-action@74a5d142397b4f367a81961eba4e8cd7edddf772 # v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Check if this is the highest release tag
        id: check_latest
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          CURRENT="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
          LATEST=$(gh api "repos/${{ github.repository }}/tags" --paginate --jq '.[].name' \
            | grep -E '^[0-9]{8}\.[0-9]{2}$' \
            | sort | tail -1)
          if [ "$CURRENT" = "$LATEST" ]; then
            echo "is_latest=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "is_latest=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "::notice::Skipping floating tags: ${CURRENT} is not the highest version (${LATEST} is)"
          fi

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@902fa8ec7d6ecbf8d84d538b9b233a880e428804 # v5
        with:
          images: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}
          tags: |
            type=match,pattern=\d{8}\.\d{2}
            type=sha
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ steps.check_latest.outputs.is_latest }}

      - name: Create multi-arch manifest and push
        env:
          TAGS: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
        working-directory: /tmp/digests
        run: |
          mapfile -t tags < <(jq -r '.tags[]' <<< "$DOCKER_METADATA_OUTPUT_JSON")
          tag_args=()
          for tag in "${tags[@]}"; do
            tag_args+=("-t" "$tag")
          done

          image_refs=()
          for digest in *; do
            image_refs+=("ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}@sha256:${digest}")
          done

          docker buildx imagetools create "${tag_args[@]}" "${image_refs[@]}"

      - name: Inspect manifest
        env:
          TAGS: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
        run: |
          tag=$(echo "$TAGS" | head -1)
          docker buildx imagetools inspect "$tag"

      - name: Get manifest digest
        id: manifest
        env:
          TAGS: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
        run: |
          tag=$(echo "$TAGS" | head -1)
          digest=$(docker buildx imagetools inspect "$tag" --format '{{json .Manifest}}' | jq -r '.digest')
          echo "digest=${digest}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Sign container image with cosign (keyless)
        if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'
        env:
          DIGEST: ${{ steps.manifest.outputs.digest }}
          TAGS: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
        run: |
          mapfile -t tags < <(printf '%s\n' "$TAGS")
          images=()
          for tag in "${tags[@]}"; do
            [ -n "$tag" ] || continue
            images+=("${tag}@${DIGEST}")
          done
          cosign sign --yes "${images[@]}"

      - name: Verify container signature
        if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'
        env:
          DIGEST: ${{ steps.manifest.outputs.digest }}
        run: |
          cosign verify \
            --certificate-identity-regexp="https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/*" \
            --certificate-oidc-issuer="https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com" \
            "ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}@${DIGEST}"

  upload-release:
    needs:
      - build-deb
      - build-rpm
      - build-arch

file.content:
name: Sign with Sigstore
description: Generate checksums and sign files with Sigstore keyless signing

inputs:
  files:
    description: Glob pattern or space-separated list of files to sign
    required: true
  working-directory:
    description: Directory containing files (defaults to current directory)
    required: false
    default: '.'
  skip-sha512:
    description: Skip SHA512 checksum generation (only generate SHA256)
    required: false
    default: 'false'

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - shell: bash
      working-directory: ${{ inputs.working-directory }}
      env:
        FILES_PATTERN: ${{ inputs.files }}
        SKIP_SHA512: ${{ inputs.skip-sha512 }}
      run: |
        # Function to generate checksum (handles macOS vs Linux)
        checksum256() {
          sha256sum "$1" > "${1}.sha256" 2>/dev/null || shasum -a 256 "$1" > "${1}.sha256"
        }
        checksum512() {
          sha512sum "$1" > "${1}.sha512" 2>/dev/null || shasum -a 512 "$1" > "${1}.sha512"
        }

        # Process each file matching the pattern
        for file in $FILES_PATTERN; do
          if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
            echo "Warning: No files matching '$file'"
            continue
          fi
          echo "Signing: $file"

          # Generate checksums
          checksum256 "$file"
          if [ "$SKIP_SHA512" != "true" ]; then
            checksum512 "$file"
          fi

          # Sign with Sigstore (keyless)
          cosign sign-blob --yes \
            --output-signature "${file}.sig" \
            --output-certificate "${file}.crt" \
            "$file"
        done

739→  build-homebrew-binaries:
   740→    needs: [clippy, test, e2e]
   741→    strategy:
   742→      matrix:
   743→        include:
   744→          - target: x86_64-apple-darwin
   745→            os: macos-latest
   746→          - target: aarch64-apple-darwin
   747→            os: macos-latest
   748→          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
   749→            os: ubuntu-22.04
   750→          - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
   751→            os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
   752→
   753→    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
   754→    name: Build binary (${{ matrix.target }})
   755→    permissions:
   756→      contents: read
   757→      id-token: write # Required for Sigstore keyless signing
   758→
   759→    steps:
   760→      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
   761→        with:
   762→          persist-credentials: false
   763→
   764→      - name: Install Rust toolchain
   765→        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@f7ccc83f9ed1e5b9c81d8a67d7ad1a747e22a561 # master
   766→        with:
   767→          toolchain: ${{ env.NIGHTLY_TOOLCHAIN }}
   768→          targets: ${{ matrix.target }}, wasm32-wasip2
   769→
   770→      - name: Install cosign
   771→        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
   772→        uses: sigstore/cosign-installer@d7d6bc7722e3daa8354c50bcb52f4837da5e9b6a # v3.8.1
   773→
   774→      - name: Build Tailwind CSS
   775→        run: |
   776→          OS=$(uname -s)
   777→          ARCH=$(uname -m)
   778→          case "${OS}-${ARCH}" in
   779→            Linux-x86_64)  TW="tailwindcss-linux-x64";;
   780→            Linux-aarch64) TW="tailwindcss-linux-arm64";;
   781→            Darwin-arm64)  TW="tailwindcss-macos-arm64";;
   782→            Darwin-x86_64) TW="tailwindcss-macos-x64";;
   783→          esac
   784→          ./scripts/download-tailwindcss-cli.sh "$TW"
   785→          cd crates/web/ui && TAILWINDCSS="../../../$TW" ./build.sh
   786→
   787→      - name: Build WASM components
   788→        run: |
   789→          cargo build --target wasm32-wasip2 -p moltis-wasm-calc -p moltis-wasm-web-fetch -p moltis-wasm-web-search --release
   790→          cargo run -p moltis-wasm-precompile --release
   791→
   792→      - name: Determine package version
   793→        id: version
   794→        run: |
   795→          if [[ "$GITHUB_REF" == refs/tags/* ]]; then
   796→            VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
   797→          else
   798→            VERSION="0.0.0-dev"
   799→          fi
   800→          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
   801→
   802→      - name: Build release binary
   803→        env:
   804→          BUILD_TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
   805→          MOLTIS_VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
   806→        run: cargo build --release --target "$BUILD_TARGET"
   807→
   808→      - name: Package binary
   809→        env:
   810→          VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
   811→          BUILD_TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
   812→        run: |
   813→          PKG_DIR="moltis-package"
   814→          mkdir -p "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/web" "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/wasm"
   815→          cp "target/$BUILD_TARGET/release/moltis" "$PKG_DIR/moltis"
   816→          cp -R crates/web/src/assets/. "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/web/"
   817→          cp "target/wasm32-wasip2/release/moltis_wasm_calc.wasm" "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/wasm/"
   818→          cp "target/wasm32-wasip2/release/moltis_wasm_web_fetch.wasm" "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/wasm/"
   819→          cp "target/wasm32-wasip2/release/moltis_wasm_web_search.wasm" "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/wasm/"
   820→          tar czf "moltis-${VERSION}-${BUILD_TARGET}.tar.gz" -C "$PKG_DIR" moltis share
   821→
   822→      - name: Sign with Sigstore and generate checksums
   823→        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
   824→        uses: ./.github/actions/sign-artifacts
   825→        with:
   826→          files: moltis-${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-${{ matrix.target }}.tar.gz
   827→
   828→      - name: Upload binary artifact
   829→        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
   830→        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
   831→        with:
   832→          name: moltis-${{ matrix.target }}
   833→          path: |
   834→            *.tar.gz
   835→            *.tar.gz.sha256
   836→            *.tar.gz.sha512
   837→            *.tar.gz.sig
   838→            *.tar.gz.crt
   839→
   840→  build-macos-app:

file.content:
build-homebrew-binaries:
    needs: [clippy, test, e2e]
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - target: x86_64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-latest
          - target: aarch64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-latest
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            os: ubuntu-22.04
          - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
            os: ubuntu-24.04-arm

    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    name: Build binary (${{ matrix.target }})
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write # Required for Sigstore keyless signing

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Install Rust toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@f7ccc83f9ed1e5b9c81d8a67d7ad1a747e22a561 # master
        with:
          toolchain: ${{ env.NIGHTLY_TOOLCHAIN }}
          targets: ${{ matrix.target }}, wasm32-wasip2

      - name: Install cosign
        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
        uses: sigstore/cosign-installer@d7d6bc7722e3daa8354c50bcb52f4837da5e9b6a # v3.8.1

      - name: Build Tailwind CSS
        run: |
          OS=$(uname -s)
          ARCH=$(uname -m)
          case "${OS}-${ARCH}" in
            Linux-x86_64)  TW="tailwindcss-linux-x64";;
            Linux-aarch64) TW="tailwindcss-linux-arm64";;
            Darwin-arm64)  TW="tailwindcss-macos-arm64";;
            Darwin-x86_64) TW="tailwindcss-macos-x64";;
          esac
          ./scripts/download-tailwindcss-cli.sh "$TW"
          cd crates/web/ui && TAILWINDCSS="../../../$TW" ./build.sh

      - name: Build WASM components
        run: |
          cargo build --target wasm32-wasip2 -p moltis-wasm-calc -p moltis-wasm-web-fetch -p moltis-wasm-web-search --release
          cargo run -p moltis-wasm-precompile --release

      - name: Determine package version
        id: version
        run: |
          if [[ "$GITHUB_REF" == refs/tags/* ]]; then
            VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
          else
            VERSION="0.0.0-dev"
          fi
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Build release binary
        env:
          BUILD_TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
          MOLTIS_VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
        run: cargo build --release --target "$BUILD_TARGET"

      - name: Package binary
        env:
          VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
          BUILD_TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
        run: |
          PKG_DIR="moltis-package"
          mkdir -p "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/web" "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/wasm"
          cp "target/$BUILD_TARGET/release/moltis" "$PKG_DIR/moltis"
          cp -R crates/web/src/assets/. "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/web/"
          cp "target/wasm32-wasip2/release/moltis_wasm_calc.wasm" "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/wasm/"
          cp "target/wasm32-wasip2/release/moltis_wasm_web_fetch.wasm" "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/wasm/"
          cp "target/wasm32-wasip2/release/moltis_wasm_web_search.wasm" "$PKG_DIR/share/moltis/wasm/"
          tar czf "moltis-${VERSION}-${BUILD_TARGET}.tar.gz" -C "$PKG_DIR" moltis share

      - name: Sign with Sigstore and generate checksums
        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
        uses: ./.github/actions/sign-artifacts
        with:
          files: moltis-${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-${{ matrix.target }}.tar.gz

      - name: Upload binary artifact
        if: ${{ env.RELEASE_DRY_RUN != 'true' }}
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
        with:
          name: moltis-${{ matrix.target }}
          path: |
            *.tar.gz
            *.tar.gz.sha256
            *.tar.gz.sha512
            *.tar.gz.sig
            *.tar.gz.crt

  build-macos-app:
    needs: [clippy, test, e2e]
    runs-on: macos-latest
    name: Build macOS app
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write # Required for Sigstore keyless signing

    steps:

   109→
   110→## Security
   111→
   112→- **Zero `unsafe` code\*** — denied workspace-wide; only opt-in FFI behind `local-embeddings` flag
   113→- **Sandboxed execution** — Docker + Apple Container, per-session isolation
   114→- **Secret handling** — `secrecy::Secret`, zeroed on drop, redacted from tool output
   115→- **Authentication** — password + passkey (WebAuthn), rate-limited, per-IP throttle
   116→- **SSRF protection** — DNS-resolved, blocks loopback/private/link-local
   117→- **Origin validation** — rejects cross-origin WebSocket upgrades
   118→- **Hook gating** — `BeforeToolCall` hooks can inspect/block any tool invocation

file.content:
Use `--no-default-features --features lightweight` for constrained devices (Raspberry Pi, etc.).

## Security

- **Zero `unsafe` code\*** — denied workspace-wide; only opt-in FFI behind `local-embeddings` flag
- **Sandboxed execution** — Docker + Apple Container, per-session isolation
- **Secret handling** — `secrecy::Secret`, zeroed on drop, redacted from tool output
- **Authentication** — password + passkey (WebAuthn), rate-limited, per-IP throttle
- **SSRF protection** — DNS-resolved, blocks loopback/private/link-local
- **Origin validation** — rejects cross-origin WebSocket upgrades
- **Hook gating** — `BeforeToolCall` hooks can inspect/block any tool invocation

See [Security Architecture](https://docs.moltis.org/security.html) for details.

## Features

file.content:
if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !(github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.dry_run)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Upload release assets
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
          sparse-checkout: CHANGELOG.md
          sparse-checkout-cone-mode: false

      - name: Download all build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@d3f86a106a0bac45b974a628896c90dbdf5c8093 # v4
        with:
          path: artifacts
          pattern: moltis-*

      - name: Download snap artifact
        uses: actions/download-artifact@d3f86a106a0bac45b974a628896c90dbdf5c8093 # v4
        with:
          name: moltis.snap
          path: artifacts/moltis.snap

      - name: Collect all release files
        run: |
          mkdir -p release-files
          find artifacts -type f \( \
            -name '*.deb' -o -name '*.rpm' -o -name '*.pkg.tar.zst' \
            -o -name '*.AppImage' -o -name '*.snap' -o -name '*.tar.gz' -o -name '*.zip' -o -name '*.exe' \
            -o -name '*.sha256' -o -name '*.sha512' -o -name '*.sig' -o -name '*.crt' \
          \) -exec cp {} release-files/ \;
          cp CHANGELOG.md release-files/
          echo "Files to upload:"
          ls -lh release-files/

      - name: Upload to release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@a06a81a03ee405af7f2048a818ed3f03bbf83c7b # v2
        with:
          files: release-files/*
          prerelease: ${{ env.RELEASE_PRE_RELEASE == 'true' }}

  # Generate SBOM for the entire release
  generate-sbom:
